authors_text: Esther Puyol-Anton,Bram Ruijsink,Stefan K. Piechnik,Stefan Neubauer,Steffen E. Petersen,Reza Razavi,Andrew P. King
title: Fairness in Cardiac MR Image Analysis: An Investigation of Bias Due to Data Imbalance in Deep Learning Based Segmentation
title_zh: 心脏 MR 图像分析的公平性：基于深度学习的分割中数据不平衡造成的偏差的调查
abstract: The subject of "fairness" in artificial intelligence (AI) refers to assessing AI algorithms for potential bias based on demographic characteristics such as race and gender, and the development of algorithms to address this bias. Most applications to date have been in computer vision, although some work in healthcare has started to emerge. The use of deep learning (DL) in cardiac MR segmentation has led to impressive results in recent years, and such techniques are starting to be translated into clinical practice. However, no work has yet investigated the fairness of such models. In this work, we perform such an analysis for racial/gender groups, focusing on the problem of training data imbalance, using a nnU-Net model trained and evaluated on cine short axis cardiac MR data from the UK Biobank dataset, consisting of 5,903 subjects from 6 different racial groups. We find statistically significant differences in Dice performance between different racial groups. To reduce the racial bias, we investigated three strategies: (1) stratified batch sampling, in which batch sampling is stratified to ensure balance between racial groups; (2) fair meta-learning for segmentation, in which a DL classifier is trained to classify race and jointly optimized with the segmentation model; and (3) protected group models, in which a different segmentation model is trained for each racial group. We also compared the results to the scenario where we have a perfectly balanced database. To assess fairness we used the standard deviation (SD) and skewed error ratio (SER) of the average Dice values. Our results demonstrate that the racial bias results from the use of imbalanced training data, and that all proposed bias mitigation strategies improved fairness, with the best SD and SER resulting from the use of protected group models.
summary: The subject of "fairness" in artificial intelligence (AI) refers to assessing AI algorithms for potential bias based on demographic characteristics such as race and gender, and the development of algorithms to address this bias. Most applications to date have been in computer vision, although some work in healthcare has started to emerge. The use of deep learning (DL) in cardiac MR segmentation has led to impressive results in recent years, and such techniques are starting to be translated into clinical practice. However, no work has yet investigated the fairness of such models. In this work, we perform such an analysis for racial/gender groups, focusing on the problem of training data imbalance, using a nnU-Net model trained and evaluated on cine short axis cardiac MR data from the UK Biobank dataset, consisting of 5,903 subjects from 6 different racial groups. We find statistically significant differences in Dice performance between different racial groups. To reduce the racial bias, we investigated three strategies: (1) stratified batch sampling, in which batch sampling is stratified to ensure balance between racial groups; (2) fair meta-learning for segmentation, in which a DL classifier is trained to classify race and jointly optimized with the segmentation model; and (3) protected group models, in which a different segmentation model is trained for each racial group. We also compared the results to the scenario where we have a perfectly balanced database. To assess fairness we used the standard deviation (SD) and skewed error ratio (SER) of the average Dice values. Our results demonstrate that the racial bias results from the use of imbalanced training data, and that all proposed bias mitigation strategies improved fairness, with the best SD and SER resulting from the use of protected group models.
abstract_zh: 人工智能（AI）中的“公平”主题是指根据种族和性别等人口特征评估人工智能算法是否存在潜在偏见，以及开发解决这种偏见的算法。迄今为止，大多数应用都在计算机视觉领域，尽管医疗保健领域的一些工作已经开始出现。近年来，深度学习 (DL) 在心脏 MR 分割中的应用取得了令人印象深刻的成果，并且此类技术正开始转化为临床实践。然而，目前还没有研究研究此类模型的公平性。在这项工作中，我们对种族/性别群体进行了这样的分析，重点关注训练数据不平衡的问题，使用 nnU-Net 模型对来自英国生物银行数据集的电影短轴心脏 MR 数据进行训练和评估，该数据集由来自 6 个不同种族群体的 5,903 名受试者组成。我们发现不同种族群体之间的 Dice 表现存在统计上的显着差异。为了减少种族偏见，我们研究了三种策略：（1）分层批量抽样，其中对批量抽样进行分层以确保种族群体之间的平衡； （2）用于分割的公平元学习，其中训练DL分类器对种族进行分类并与分割模型联合优化； (3)保护群体模型，其中为每个种族群体训练不同的分割模型。我们还将结果与我们拥有完美平衡数据库的场景进行了比较。为了评估公平性，我们使用平均 Dice 值的标准差 (SD) 和偏斜误差率 (SER)。我们的结果表明，种族偏见是由于使用不平衡的训练数据造成的，并且所有提出的偏见缓解策略都提高了公平性，最佳的 SD 和 SER 是由于使用受保护群体模型而产生的。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了心脏磁共振图像分割中因数据不平衡导致的种族/性别偏见问题，并提出了三种缓解策略。
AI.motivation: 尽管深度学习在心脏MRI分割中取得进展，但尚未有研究探讨模型在不同种族/性别群体中的公平性，而数据不平衡可能导致算法偏见。
AI.method: 基于UK Biobank数据集，使用nnU-Net模型分析种族/性别分组的Dice值差异，并通过分层批量采样、公平元学习和分组模型三种策略减少偏见。
AI.result: 发现种族间Dice值存在统计学差异，三种策略均提升公平性，其中分组模型在标准差和偏误比指标上表现最佳。
AI.conclusion: 数据不平衡是导致种族偏见的主要原因，分组模型最有效，但需权衡临床实用性与公平性需求。

title: AC/DC: Alternating Compressed/DeCompressed Training of Deep Neural Networks
title_zh: AC/DC：深度神经网络的交替压缩/解压缩训练
abstract: The increasing computational requirements of deep neural networks (DNNs) have led to significant interest in obtaining DNN models that are sparse, yet accurate. Recent work has investigated the even harder case of sparse training, where the DNN weights are, for as much as possible, already sparse to reduce computational costs during training. Existing sparse training methods are often empirical and can have lower accuracy relative to the dense baseline. In this paper, we present a general approach called Alternating Compressed/DeCompressed (AC/DC) training of DNNs, demonstrate convergence for a variant of the algorithm, and show that AC/DC outperforms existing sparse training methods in accuracy at similar computational budgets; at high sparsity levels, AC/DC even outperforms existing methods that rely on accurate pre-trained dense models. An important property of AC/DC is that it allows co-training of dense and sparse models, yielding accurate sparse-dense model pairs at the end of the training process. This is useful in practice, where compressed variants may be desirable for deployment in resource-constrained settings without re-doing the entire training flow, and also provides us with insights into the accuracy gap between dense and compressed models. The code is available at: https://github.com/IST-DASLab/ACDC .
summary: The increasing computational requirements of deep neural networks (DNNs) have led to significant interest in obtaining DNN models that are sparse, yet accurate. Recent work has investigated the even harder case of sparse training, where the DNN weights are, for as much as possible, already sparse to reduce computational costs during training. Existing sparse training methods are often empirical and can have lower accuracy relative to the dense baseline. In this paper, we present a general approach called Alternating Compressed/DeCompressed (AC/DC) training of DNNs, demonstrate convergence for a variant of the algorithm, and show that AC/DC outperforms existing sparse training methods in accuracy at similar computational budgets; at high sparsity levels, AC/DC even outperforms existing methods that rely on accurate pre-trained dense models. An important property of AC/DC is that it allows co-training of dense and sparse models, yielding accurate sparse-dense model pairs at the end of the training process. This is useful in practice, where compressed variants may be desirable for deployment in resource-constrained settings without re-doing the entire training flow, and also provides us with insights into the accuracy gap between dense and compressed models. The code is available at: https://github.com/IST-DASLab/ACDC .
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络 (DNN) 不断增长的计算要求引发了人们对获取稀疏但准确的 DNN 模型的浓厚兴趣。最近的工作研究了更困难的稀疏训练情况，其中 DNN 权重已经尽可能稀疏，以减少训练期间的计算成本。现有的稀疏训练方法通常是经验性的，并且相对于密集基线来说精度较低。在本文中，我们提出了一种称为 DNN 交替压缩/解压缩 (AC/DC) 训练的通用方法，演示了算法变体的收敛性，并表明在相似的计算预算下，AC/DC 在准确性方面优于现有的稀疏训练方法；在高稀疏度水平下，AC/DC 甚至优于依赖于精确的预训练密集模型的现有方法。 AC/DC 的一个重要特性是它允许密集模型和稀疏模型的协同训练，在训练过程结束时产生准确的稀疏-密集模型对。这在实践中很有用，因为压缩变体可能适合在资源受限的环境中部署，而无需重新执行整个训练流程，并且还为我们提供了对密集模型和压缩模型之间的准确性差距的见解。该代码位于：https://github.com/IST-DASLab/ACDC。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为AC/DC的深度神经网络交替压缩/解压训练方法，旨在在保持模型精度的同时减少计算成本。
AI.motivation: 现有稀疏训练方法在计算成本降低的情况下精度较低，且难以与密集模型保持性能平衡，需寻找更高效的稀疏训练方案。
AI.method: 通过交替进行压缩训练和解压训练，联合训练密集模型与稀疏模型，利用压缩阶段的稀疏性约束和解压阶段的精度优化实现收敛。
AI.result: AC/DC在相似计算预算下优于现有稀疏训练方法，高稀疏度下甚至超越依赖预训练密集模型的方法，并生成可部署的稀疏-密集模型对。
AI.conclusion: AC/DC有效缩小了密集模型与压缩模型的精度差距，为资源受限场景提供了实用的模型压缩方案，代码已开源。

authors_text: Sucheng Ren,Zhengqi Gao,Tianyu Hua,Zihui Xue,Yonglong Tian,Shengfeng He,Hang Zhao
title: Co-advise: Cross Inductive Bias Distillation
title_zh: 共同建议：交叉感应偏压蒸馏
abstract: Transformers recently are adapted from the community of natural language processing as a promising substitute of convolution-based neural networks for visual learning tasks. However, its supremacy degenerates given an insufficient amount of training data (e.g., ImageNet). To make it into practical utility, we propose a novel distillation-based method to train vision transformers. Unlike previous works, where merely heavy convolution-based teachers are provided, we introduce lightweight teachers with different architectural inductive biases (e.g., convolution and involution) to co-advise the student transformer. The key is that teachers with different inductive biases attain different knowledge despite that they are trained on the same dataset, and such different knowledge compounds and boosts the student's performance during distillation. Equipped with this cross inductive bias distillation method, our vision transformers (termed as CivT) outperform all previous transformers of the same architecture on ImageNet.
summary: Transformers recently are adapted from the community of natural language processing as a promising substitute of convolution-based neural networks for visual learning tasks. However, its supremacy degenerates given an insufficient amount of training data (e.g., ImageNet). To make it into practical utility, we propose a novel distillation-based method to train vision transformers. Unlike previous works, where merely heavy convolution-based teachers are provided, we introduce lightweight teachers with different architectural inductive biases (e.g., convolution and involution) to co-advise the student transformer. The key is that teachers with different inductive biases attain different knowledge despite that they are trained on the same dataset, and such different knowledge compounds and boosts the student's performance during distillation. Equipped with this cross inductive bias distillation method, our vision transformers (termed as CivT) outperform all previous transformers of the same architecture on ImageNet.
abstract_zh: Transformers 最近从自然语言处理社区改编而来，作为视觉学习任务中基于卷积的神经网络的有希望的替代品。然而，如果训练数据量不足（例如 ImageNet），它的霸主地位就会退化。为了使其实用化，我们提出了一种新颖的基于蒸馏的方法来训练视觉变压器。与以前的工作不同，以前的工作仅提供基于重卷积的教师，我们引入了具有不同架构归纳偏差（例如卷积和对合）的轻量级教师来共同指导学生变压器。关键在于，具有不同归纳偏差的教师尽管接受相同数据集的训练，却获得了不同的知识，而这种不同的知识在蒸馏过程中会复合并提高学生的表现。配备了这种交叉归纳偏差蒸馏方法，我们的视觉 Transformer（称为 CivT）的性能优于 ImageNet 上所有先前相同架构的 Transformer。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于交叉归纳偏置蒸馏的新方法，通过轻量级教师模型（如卷积和involution）共同指导视觉Transformer（CivT），以提升其在数据不足情况下的性能。
AI.motivation: 尽管Transformer在视觉任务中表现出潜力，但其性能在训练数据不足时（如ImageNet）会显著下降，现有蒸馏方法依赖单一重型卷积教师模型，限制了性能提升。
AI.method: 引入具有不同架构归纳偏置（如卷积和involution）的轻量级教师模型，通过多教师协同蒸馏机制，将不同知识整合到学生Transformer中，实现性能提升。
AI.result: CivT在ImageNet上超越了所有相同架构的先前Transformer模型，验证了交叉归纳偏置蒸馏的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过多教师模型的协同知识蒸馏，交叉归纳偏置方法能有效提升视觉Transformer性能，为数据有限场景下的模型训练提供了新思路。

However, its supremacy degenerates given an insufficient amount of training data (e.g., ImageNet). Unlike previous works, where merely heavy convolution-based teachers are provided, we introduce lightweight teachers with different architectural inductive biases (e.g., convolution and involution) to co-advise the student transformer. Equipped with this cross inductive bias distillation method, our vision transformers (termed as CivT) outperform all previous transformers of the same architecture on ImageNet. authors_text: Jiajie Zou,Yuran Zhang,Peiqing Jin,Cheng Luo,Xunyi Pan,Nai Ding
title: PALRACE: Reading Comprehension Dataset with Human Data and Labeled Rationales
title_zh: PALRACE：包含人类数据和标记原理的阅读理解数据集
abstract: Pre-trained language models achieves high performance on machine reading comprehension (MRC) tasks but the results are hard to explain. An appealing approach to make models explainable is to provide rationales for its decision. To investigate whether human rationales can further improve current models and to facilitate supervised learning of human rationales, here we present PALRACE (Pruned And Labeled RACE), a new MRC dataset with human labeled rationales for 800 passages selected from the RACE dataset. We further classified the question to each passage into 6 types. Each passage was read by at least 26 human readers, who labeled their rationales to answer the question. It is demonstrated that models such as RoBERTa-large outperforms human readers in all 6 types of questions, including inference questions, but its performance can be further improved when having access to the human rationales. Simpler models and pre-trained models that are not fine-tuned based on the task benefit more from human rationales, and their performance can be boosted by more than 30% by rationales. With access to human rationales, a simple model based on the GloVe word embedding can reach the performance of BERT-base.
summary: Pre-trained language models achieves high performance on machine reading comprehension (MRC) tasks but the results are hard to explain. An appealing approach to make models explainable is to provide rationales for its decision. To investigate whether human rationales can further improve current models and to facilitate supervised learning of human rationales, here we present PALRACE (Pruned And Labeled RACE), a new MRC dataset with human labeled rationales for 800 passages selected from the RACE dataset. We further classified the question to each passage into 6 types. Each passage was read by at least 26 human readers, who labeled their rationales to answer the question. It is demonstrated that models such as RoBERTa-large outperforms human readers in all 6 types of questions, including inference questions, but its performance can be further improved when having access to the human rationales. Simpler models and pre-trained models that are not fine-tuned based on the task benefit more from human rationales, and their performance can be boosted by more than 30% by rationales. With access to human rationales, a simple model based on the GloVe word embedding can reach the performance of BERT-base.
abstract_zh: 预训练的语言模型在机器阅读理解（MRC）任务上实现了高性能，但结果很难解释。使模型可解释的一个有吸引力的方法是为其决策提供理由。为了研究人类基本原理是否可以进一步改进当前模型并促进对人类基本原理的监督学习，我们在这里提出了 PALRACE（修剪和标记的 RACE），这是一个新的 MRC 数据集，其中包含从 RACE 数据集中选择的 800 个段落的人类标记基本原理。我们进一步将每篇文章的问题分为 6 种类型。每篇文章都由至少 26 名人类读者阅读，他们标记了回答问题的理由。事实证明，像 RoBERTa-large 这样的模型在包括推理问题在内的所有 6 种类型的问题上都优于人类读者，但当能够获得人类的基本原理时，其性能可以进一步提高。更简单的模型和没有根据任务进行微调的预训练模型更多地受益于人类理性，其性能可以通过理性提升30%以上。通过了解人类的基本原理，基于 GloVe 词嵌入的简单模型可以达到 BERT-base 的性能。

AI.tldr: 构建包含人类标注推理依据的阅读理解数据集PALRACE，用于提升模型可解释性与性能。
AI.motivation: 预训练语言模型在阅读理解任务中表现优异但缺乏可解释性，需通过人类标注的推理依据增强模型透明度并促进监督学习。
AI.method: 从RACE数据集中选取800篇文本，按问题类型分类，由至少26名人类标注者为每段文本标注答题依据，形成包含6类问题的标注数据集。
AI.result: RoBERTa-large在6类问题上均优于人类，模型借助人类标注依据后性能显著提升，简单模型（如GloVe）结合依据可达到BERT-base水平，部分模型性能提升超30%。
AI.conclusion: 人类标注的推理依据能有效提升模型性能，尤其对简单模型效果显著，未来可进一步探索依据利用机制以优化模型设计。

search_text: PALRACE: Reading Comprehension Dataset with Human Data and Labeled Rationales PALRACE：包含人类数据和标记原理的阅读理解数据集 Pre-trained language models achieves high performance on machine reading comprehension (MRC) tasks but the results are hard to explain. An appealing approach to make models explainable is to provide rationales for its decision. To investigate whether human rationales can further improve current models and to facilitate supervised learning of human rationales, here we present PALRACE (Pruned And Labeled RACE), a new MRC dataset with human labeled rationales for 800 passages selected from the RACE dataset. We further classified the question to each passage into 6 types. Each passage was read by at least 26 human readers, who labeled their rationales to answer the question. It is demonstrated that models such as RoBERTa-large outperforms human readers in all 6 types of questions, including inference questions, but its performance can be further improved when having access to the human rationales. Simpler models and pre-trained models that are not fine-tuned based on the task benefit more from human rationales, and their performance can be boosted by more than 30% by rationales. With access to human rationales, a simple model based on the GloVe word embedding can reach the performance of BERT-base. 预训练的语言模型在机器阅读理解（MRC）任务上实现了高性能，但结果很难解释。使模型可解释的一个有吸引力的方法是为其决策提供理由。为了研究人类基本原理是否可以进一步改进当前模型并促进对人类基本原理的监督学习，我们在这里提出了 PALRACE（修剪和标记的 RACE），这是一个新的 MRC 数据集，其中包含从 RACE 数据集中选择的 800 个段落的人类标记基本原理。我们进一步将每篇文章的问题分为 6 种类型。每篇文章都由至少 26 名人类读者阅读，他们标记了回答问题的理由。事实证明，像 RoBERTa-large 这样的模型在包括推理问题在内的所有 6 种类型的问题上都优于人类读者，但当能够获得人类的基本原理时，其性能可以进一步提高。更简单的模型和没有根据任务进行微调的预训练模型更多地受益于人类理性，其性能可以通过理性提升30%以上。通过了解人类的基本原理，基于 GloVe 词嵌入的简单模型可以达到 BERT-base 的性能。 构建包含人类标注推理依据的阅读理解数据集PALRACE，用于提升模型可解释性与性能。 Jiajie Zou,Yuran Zhang,Peiqing Jin,Cheng Luo,Xunyi Pan,Nai Ding cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Qibin Hou,Zihang Jiang,Li Yuan,Ming-Ming Cheng,Shuicheng Yan,Jiashi Feng
title: Vision Permutator: A Permutable MLP-Like Architecture for Visual Recognition
title_zh: 视觉置换器：用于视觉识别的可置换类 MLP 架构
abstract: In this paper, we present Vision Permutator, a conceptually simple and data efficient MLP-like architecture for visual recognition. By realizing the importance of the positional information carried by 2D feature representations, unlike recent MLP-like models that encode the spatial information along the flattened spatial dimensions, Vision Permutator separately encodes the feature representations along the height and width dimensions with linear projections. This allows Vision Permutator to capture long-range dependencies along one spatial direction and meanwhile preserve precise positional information along the other direction. The resulting position-sensitive outputs are then aggregated in a mutually complementing manner to form expressive representations of the objects of interest. We show that our Vision Permutators are formidable competitors to convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers. Without the dependence on spatial convolutions or attention mechanisms, Vision Permutator achieves 81.5% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet without extra large-scale training data (e.g., ImageNet-22k) using only 25M learnable parameters, which is much better than most CNNs and vision transformers under the same model size constraint. When scaling up to 88M, it attains 83.2% top-1 accuracy. We hope this work could encourage research on rethinking the way of encoding spatial information and facilitate the development of MLP-like models. Code is available at https://github.com/Andrew-Qibin/VisionPermutator.
summary: In this paper, we present Vision Permutator, a conceptually simple and data efficient MLP-like architecture for visual recognition. By realizing the importance of the positional information carried by 2D feature representations, unlike recent MLP-like models that encode the spatial information along the flattened spatial dimensions, Vision Permutator separately encodes the feature representations along the height and width dimensions with linear projections. This allows Vision Permutator to capture long-range dependencies along one spatial direction and meanwhile preserve precise positional information along the other direction. The resulting position-sensitive outputs are then aggregated in a mutually complementing manner to form expressive representations of the objects of interest. We show that our Vision Permutators are formidable competitors to convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers. Without the dependence on spatial convolutions or attention mechanisms, Vision Permutator achieves 81.5% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet without extra large-scale training data (e.g., ImageNet-22k) using only 25M learnable parameters, which is much better than most CNNs and vision transformers under the same model size constraint. When scaling up to 88M, it attains 83.2% top-1 accuracy. We hope this work could encourage research on rethinking the way of encoding spatial information and facilitate the development of MLP-like models. Code is available at https://github.com/Andrew-Qibin/VisionPermutator.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 Vision Permutator，这是一种概念简单且数据高效的类似 MLP 的视觉识别架构。通过认识到 2D 特征表示所携带的位置信息的重要性，与最近沿扁平空间维度编码空间信息的类 MLP 模型不同，Vision Permutator 使用线性投影沿高度和宽度维度分别编码特征表示。这使得 Vision Permutator 能够捕获沿一个空间方向的远程依赖性，同时保留沿另一方向的精确位置信息。然后以相互补充的方式聚合所得的位置敏感输出，以形成感兴趣对象的表达表示。我们表明，我们的视觉排列器是卷积神经网络（CNN）和视觉变换器的强大竞争对手。在不依赖空间卷积或注意力机制的情况下，Vision Permutator 在 ImageNet 上无需额外的大规模训练数据（例如 ImageNet-22k），仅使用 25M 可学习参数即可实现 81.5% 的 top-1 准确率，这比相同模型大小约束下的大多数 CNN 和视觉 Transformer 好得多。当扩展到88M时，它达到了83.2%的top-1精度。我们希望这项工作能够鼓励重新思考空间信息编码方式的研究，并促进类 MLP 模型的开发。代码可在 https://github.com/Andrew-Qibin/VisionPermutator 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Vision Permutator的视觉识别MLP-like架构，通过独立编码高度和宽度维度来捕捉长距离依赖并保留位置信息。
AI.motivation: 现有MLP-like模型在处理空间信息时依赖于展平的维度，导致位置信息丢失，而本文旨在通过分离编码高度和宽度来改进这一问题。
AI.method: 通过线性投影分别对高度和宽度维度进行编码，再以互补方式聚合位置敏感输出，从而在保持精确位置信息的同时捕捉长距离依赖。
AI.result: 在ImageNet数据集上，使用25M参数达到81.5%的top-1准确率，88M参数时达到83.2%，优于同规模的CNN和视觉Transformer。
AI.conclusion: Vision Permutator证明了无需卷积或注意力机制即可高效编码空间信息，为MLP-like模型的发展提供了新思路。

search_text: Vision Permutator: A Permutable MLP-Like Architecture for Visual Recognition 视觉置换器：用于视觉识别的可置换类 MLP 架构 In this paper, we present Vision Permutator, a conceptually simple and data efficient MLP-like architecture for visual recognition. By realizing the importance of the positional information carried by 2D feature representations, unlike recent MLP-like models that encode the spatial information along the flattened spatial dimensions, Vision Permutator separately encodes the feature representations along the height and width dimensions with linear projections. This allows Vision Permutator to capture long-range dependencies along one spatial direction and meanwhile preserve precise positional information along the other direction. The resulting position-sensitive outputs are then aggregated in a mutually complementing manner to form expressive representations of the objects of interest. We show that our Vision Permutators are formidable competitors to convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers. Without the dependence on spatial convolutions or attention mechanisms, Vision Permutator achieves 81.5% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet without extra large-scale training data (e.g., ImageNet-22k) using only 25M learnable parameters, which is much better than most CNNs and vision transformers under the same model size constraint. When scaling up to 88M, it attains 83.2% top-1 accuracy. We hope this work could encourage research on rethinking the way of encoding spatial information and facilitate the development of MLP-like models. Code is available at https://github.com/Andrew-Qibin/VisionPermutator. 在本文中，我们提出了 Vision Permutator，这是一种概念简单且数据高效的类似 MLP 的视觉识别架构。通过认识到 2D 特征表示所携带的位置信息的重要性，与最近沿扁平空间维度编码空间信息的类 MLP 模型不同，Vision Permutator 使用线性投影沿高度和宽度维度分别编码特征表示。这使得 Vision Permutator 能够捕获沿一个空间方向的远程依赖性，同时保留沿另一方向的精确位置信息。然后以相互补充的方式聚合所得的位置敏感输出，以形成感兴趣对象的表达表示。我们表明，我们的视觉排列器是卷积神经网络（CNN）和视觉变换器的强大竞争对手。在不依赖空间卷积或注意力机制的情况下，Vision Permutator 在 ImageNet 上无需额外的大规模训练数据（例如 ImageNet-22k），仅使用 25M 可学习参数即可实现 81.5% 的 top-1 准确率，这比相同模型大小约束下的大多数 CNN 和视觉 Transformer 好得多。当扩展到88M时，它达到了83.2%的top-1精度。我们希望这项工作能够鼓励重新思考空间信息编码方式的研究，并促进类 MLP 模型的开发。代码可在 https://github.com/Andrew-Qibin/VisionPermutator 获取。 本文提出了一种名为Vision Permutator的视觉识别MLP-like架构，通过独立编码高度和宽度维度来捕捉长距离依赖并保留位置信息。 Qibin Hou,Zihang Jiang,Li Yuan,Ming-Ming Cheng,Shuicheng Yan,Jiashi Feng cs.CV

title: A new Video Synopsis Based Approach Using Stereo Camera
title_zh: 一种使用立体相机的基于视频概要的新方法
abstract: In today's world, the amount of data produced in every field has increased at an unexpected level. In the face of increasing data, the importance of data processing has increased remarkably. Our resource topic is on the processing of video data, which has an important place in increasing data, and the production of summary videos. Within the scope of this resource, a new method for anomaly detection with object-based unsupervised learning has been developed while creating a video summary. By using this method, the video data is processed as pixels and the result is produced as a video segment. The process flow can be briefly summarized as follows. Objects on the video are detected according to their type, and then they are tracked. Then, the tracking history data of the objects are processed, and the classifier is trained with the object type. Thanks to this classifier, anomaly behavior of objects is detected. Video segments are determined by processing video moments containing anomaly behaviors. The video summary is created by extracting the detected video segments from the original video and combining them. The model we developed has been tested and verified separately for single camera and dual camera systems.

By using this method, the video data is processed as pixels and the result is produced as a video segment. The process flow can be briefly summarized as follows. Objects on the video are detected according to their type, and then they are tracked. Then, the tracking history data of the objects are processed, and the classifier is trained with the object type. Thanks to this classifier, anomaly behavior of objects is detected. Video segments are determined by processing video moments containing anomaly behaviors. The video summary is created by extracting the detected video segments from the original video and combining them. The model we developed has been tested and verified separately for single camera and dual camera systems.
abstract_zh: 当今世界，各个领域产生的数据量都以意想不到的水平增长。面对不断增加的数据，数据处理的重要性显着增加。我们的资源主题是视频数据的处理，这在增加数据和摘要视频的制作方面具有重要地位。在此资源的范围内，在创建视频摘要时开发了一种使用基于对象的无监督学习进行异常检测的新方法。通过使用这种方法，视频数据被处理为像素，并且结果被生成为视频片段。工艺流程可简要概括如下。根据其类型检测视频中的对象，然后对其进行跟踪。然后，处理对象的跟踪历史数据，并用对象类型训练分类器。借助这个分类器，可以检测到对象的异常行为。通过处理包含异常行为的视频时刻来确定视频片段。视频摘要是通过从原始视频中提取检测到的视频片段并将它们组合来创建的。我们开发的模型已经针对单摄像头和双摄像头系统分别进行了测试和验证。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于立体相机的新视频摘要方法，结合基于对象的无监督学习进行异常检测。
AI.motivation: 随着数据量激增，视频数据处理需求提升，传统方法难以高效生成视频摘要并检测异常行为。
AI.method: 通过对象检测与跟踪、历史数据处理及分类器训练，利用异常行为检测确定视频片段，最终生成摘要视频。
AI.result: 模型分别在单摄像头和双摄像头系统中测试验证，有效提取异常片段并生成视频摘要。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过立体相机与无监督学习结合，实现了高效的视频摘要生成与异常行为检测。

title: Small tile sets that compute while solving mazes
title_zh: 在解决迷宫时进行计算的小型图块集
comment: 18 pages. 7 figures. 1 appendix. To appear at the 27th International Conference on DNA Computing and Molecular Programming (DNA27)
comments: 18 pages. 7 figures. 1 appendix. To appear at the 27th International Conference on DNA Computing and Molecular Programming (DNA27)
abstract: We ask the question of how small a self-assembling set of tiles can be yet have interesting computational behaviour. We study this question in a model where supporting walls are provided as an input structure for tiles to grow along: we call it the Maze-Walking Tile Assembly Model. The model has a number of implementation prospects, one being DNA strands that attach to a DNA origami substrate. Intuitively, the model suggests a separation of signal routing and computation: the input structure (maze) supplies a routing diagram, and the programmer's tile set provides the computational ability. We ask how simple the computational part can be. We give two tiny tile sets that are computationally universal in the Maze-Walking Tile Assembly Model. The first has four tiles and simulates Boolean circuits by directly implementing NAND, NXOR and NOT gates. Our second tile set has 6 tiles and is called the Collatz tile set as it produces patterns found in binary/ternary representations of iterations of the Collatz function. Using computer search we find that the Collatz tile set is expressive enough to encode Boolean circuits using blocks of these patterns. These two tile sets give two different methods to find simple universal tile sets, and provide motivation for using pre-assembled maze structures as circuit wiring diagrams in molecular self-assembly based computing.
summary: We ask the question of how small a self-assembling set of tiles can be yet have interesting computational behaviour. We study this question in a model where supporting walls are provided as an input structure for tiles to grow along: we call it the Maze-Walking Tile Assembly Model. The model has a number of implementation prospects, one being DNA strands that attach to a DNA origami substrate. Intuitively, the model suggests a separation of signal routing and computation: the input structure (maze) supplies a routing diagram, and the programmer's tile set provides the computational ability. We ask how simple the computational part can be. We give two tiny tile sets that are computationally universal in the Maze-Walking Tile Assembly Model. The first has four tiles and simulates Boolean circuits by directly implementing NAND, NXOR and NOT gates. Our second tile set has 6 tiles and is called the Collatz tile set as it produces patterns found in binary/ternary representations of iterations of the Collatz function. Using computer search we find that the Collatz tile set is expressive enough to encode Boolean circuits using blocks of these patterns. These two tile sets give two different methods to find simple universal tile sets, and provide motivation for using pre-assembled maze structures as circuit wiring diagrams in molecular self-assembly based computing.
abstract_zh: 我们提出的问题是，一组自组装图块可以有多小，但仍具有有趣的计算行为。我们在一个模型中研究这个问题，其中提供支撑墙作为瓷砖生长的输入结构：我们称之为迷宫行走瓷砖组装模型。该模型有多种实施前景，其中之一是附着在 DNA 折纸基底上的 DNA 链。直观上，该模型建议将信号路由和计算分开：输入结构（迷宫）提供路由图，程序员的图块集提供计算能力。我们问计算部分可以有多简单。我们给出了两个微小的图块集，它们在迷宫行走图块组装模型中具有计算通用性。第一个有四个块，通过直接实现 NAND、NXOR 和 NOT 门来模拟布尔电路。我们的第二个图块集有 6 个图块，称为 Collat​​z 图块集，因为它生成在 Collat​​z 函数迭代的二进制/三元表示中找到的模式。通过计算机搜索，我们发现 Collat​​z 图块集具有足够的表现力，可以使用这些模式的块对布尔电路进行编码。这两个图块集提供了两种不同的方法来查找简单的通用图块集，并为在基于分子自组装的计算中使用预组装的迷宫结构作为电路接线图提供了动力。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在迷宫行走瓦片自组装模型中，如何构建具有计算能力的最小瓦片集合。
AI.motivation: 探索在分子自组装计算中，如何通过预组装的迷宫结构实现简单的计算单元，以降低复杂度并提升可行性。
AI.method: 设计两种瓦片集合：一种通过直接实现逻辑门（4个瓦片），另一种利用Collatz函数模式（6个瓦片），并通过计算机搜索验证其表达能力。
AI.result: 发现两种计算通用的瓦片集合，分别能模拟布尔电路和编码布尔电路，证明了简单瓦片集合在迷宫模型中的计算潜力。
AI.conclusion: 预组装迷宫结构可作为分子计算中的电路布线图，简单瓦片集合与之结合能实现高效计算，为分子自组装计算提供新思路。

method: 设计两种瓦片集合：一种通过直接实现逻辑门（4个瓦片），另一种利用Collatz函数模式（6个瓦片），并通过计算机搜索验证其表达能力。
result: 发现两种计算通用的瓦片集合，分别能模拟布尔电路和编码布尔电路，证明了简单瓦片集合在迷宫模型中的计算潜力。
conclusion: 预组装迷宫结构可作为分子计算中的电路布线图，简单瓦片集合与之结合能实现高效计算，为分子自组装计算提供新思路。
search_text: Small tile sets that compute while solving mazes 在解决迷宫时进行计算的小型图块集 We ask the question of how small a self-assembling set of tiles can be yet have interesting computational behaviour. We study this question in a model where supporting walls are provided as an input structure for tiles to grow along: we call it the Maze-Walking Tile Assembly Model. The model has a number of implementation prospects, one being DNA strands that attach to a DNA origami substrate. Intuitively, the model suggests a separation of signal routing and computation: the input structure (maze) supplies a routing diagram, and the programmer's tile set provides the computational ability. We ask how simple the computational part can be. We give two tiny tile sets that are computationally universal in the Maze-Walking Tile Assembly Model. The first has four tiles and simulates Boolean circuits by directly implementing NAND, NXOR and NOT gates. Our second tile set has 6 tiles and is called the Collatz tile set as it produces patterns found in binary/ternary representations of iterations of the Collatz function. Using computer search we find that the Collatz tile set is expressive enough to encode Boolean circuits using blocks of these patterns. These two tile sets give two different methods to find simple universal tile sets, and provide motivation for using pre-assembled maze structures as circuit wiring diagrams in molecular self-assembly based computing. 我们提出的问题是，一组自组装图块可以有多小，但仍具有有趣的计算行为。我们在一个模型中研究这个问题，其中提供支撑墙作为瓷砖生长的输入结构：我们称之为迷宫行走瓷砖组装模型。该模型有多种实施前景，其中之一是附着在 DNA 折纸基底上的 DNA 链。直观上，该模型建议将信号路由和计算分开：输入结构（迷宫）提供路由图，程序员的图块集提供计算能力。我们问计算部分可以有多简单。我们给出了两个微小的图块集，它们在迷宫行走图块组装模型中具有计算通用性。第一个有四个块，通过直接实现 NAND、NXOR 和 NOT 门来模拟布尔电路。我们的第二个图块集有 6 个图块，称为 Collat​​z 图块集，因为它生成在 Collat​​z 函数迭代的二进制/三元表示中找到的模式。通过计算机搜索，我们发现 Collat​​z 图块集具有足够的表现力，可以使用这些模式的块对布尔电路进行编码。这两个图块集提供了两种不同的方法来查找简单的通用图块集，并为在基于分子自组装的计算中使用预组装的迷宫结构作为电路接线图提供了动力。 本文研究了在迷宫行走瓦片自组装模型中，如何构建具有计算能力的最小瓦片集合。 Matthew Cook,Tristan Stérin,Damien Woods cs.ET,cs.CC,cs.DM

title: Open Images V5 Text Annotation and Yet Another Mask Text Spotter
title_zh: Open Images V5 文本注释和另一个蒙版文本识别器
abstract: A large scale human-labeled dataset plays an important role in creating high quality deep learning models. In this paper we present text annotation for Open Images V5 dataset. To our knowledge it is the largest among publicly available manually created text annotations. Having this annotation we trained a simple Mask-RCNN-based network, referred as Yet Another Mask Text Spotter (YAMTS), which achieves competitive performance or even outperforms current state-of-the-art approaches in some cases on ICDAR2013, ICDAR2015 and Total-Text datasets. Code for text spotting model available online at: https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/training_extensions. The model can be exported to OpenVINO-format and run on Intel CPUs.
summary: A large scale human-labeled dataset plays an important role in creating high quality deep learning models. In this paper we present text annotation for Open Images V5 dataset. To our knowledge it is the largest among publicly available manually created text annotations. Having this annotation we trained a simple Mask-RCNN-based network, referred as Yet Another Mask Text Spotter (YAMTS), which achieves competitive performance or even outperforms current state-of-the-art approaches in some cases on ICDAR2013, ICDAR2015 and Total-Text datasets. Code for text spotting model available online at: https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/training_extensions. The model can be exported to OpenVINO-format and run on Intel CPUs.
abstract_zh: 大规模人工标记数据集在创建高质量深度学习模型方面发挥着重要作用。在本文中，我们提出了 Open Images V5 数据集的文本注释。据我们所知，它是公开可用的手动创建的文本注释中最大的。有了这个注释，我们训练了一个简单的基于 Mask-RCNN 的网络，称为 Yet Another Mask Text Spotter (YAMTS)，它在 ICDAR2013、ICDAR2015 和 Total-Text 数据集上实现了有竞争力的性能，甚至在某些情况下超越了当前最先进的方法。文本识别模型的代码可在线获取：https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/training_extensions。该模型可以导出为 OpenVINO 格式并在 Intel CPU 上运行。

AI.tldr: 本文主要任务是为Open Images V5数据集创建大规模文本标注，并开发名为YAMTS的文本检测模型。
AI.motivation: 大规模人工标注数据集对构建高质量深度学习模型至关重要，而现有公开数据集可能不足，因此需要创建更大规模的标注并开发高效模型。
AI.method: 基于Mask-RCNN架构，利用Open Images V5的文本标注训练Yet Another Mask Text Spotter (YAMTS)模型，优化文本检测与识别。
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上，YAMTS模型性能达到或超过当前最先进方法，且支持导出为OpenVINO格式在Intel CPU上运行。
AI.conclusion: 本文通过构建大规模文本标注数据集，开发出高效文本检测模型YAMTS，为文本识别任务提供了新的解决方案和工具。

title: CharacterChat: Supporting the Creation of Fictional Characters through Conversation and Progressive Manifestation with a Chatbot
title_zh: CharacterChat：通过聊天机器人的对话和渐进表现来支持虚构人物的创建
abstract: We present CharacterChat, a concept and chatbot to support writers in creating fictional characters. Concretely, writers progressively turn the bot into their imagined character through conversation. We iteratively developed CharacterChat in a user-centred approach, starting with a survey on character creation with writers (N=30), followed by two qualitative user studies (N=7 and N=8). Our prototype combines two modes: (1) Guided prompts help writers define character attributes (e.g. User: "Your name is Jane."), including suggestions for attributes (e.g. Bot: "What is my main motivation?") and values, realised as a rule-based system with a concept network. (2) Open conversation with the chatbot helps writers explore their character and get inspiration, realised with a language model that takes into account the defined character attributes. Our user studies reveal benefits particularly for early stages of character creation, and challenges due to limited conversational capabilities. We conclude with lessons learned and ideas for future work.
summary: We present CharacterChat, a concept and chatbot to support writers in creating fictional characters. Concretely, writers progressively turn the bot into their imagined character through conversation. We iteratively developed CharacterChat in a user-centred approach, starting with a survey on character creation with writers (N=30), followed by two qualitative user studies (N=7 and N=8). Our prototype combines two modes: (1) Guided prompts help writers define character attributes (e.g. User: "Your name is Jane."), including suggestions for attributes (e.g. Bot: "What is my main motivation?") and values, realised as a rule-based system with a concept network. (2) Open conversation with the chatbot helps writers explore their character and get inspiration, realised with a language model that takes into account the defined character attributes. Our user studies reveal benefits particularly for early stages of character creation, and challenges due to limited conversational capabilities. We conclude with lessons learned and ideas for future work.
abstract_zh: 我们推出了CharacterChat，这是一个支持作家创建虚构角色的概念和聊天机器人。具体来说，作家通过对话逐步将机器人变成他们想象的角色。我们以用户为中心的方法迭代开发了CharacterChat，首先对作家进行角色创作调查（N=30），然后进行两项定性用户研究（N=7 和N=8）。我们的原型结合了两种模式：（1）引导提示帮助作者定义角色属性（例如用户：“你的名字是简。”），包括属性建议（例如机器人：“我的主要动机是什么？”）和价值观，实现为具有概念网络的基于规则的系统。 (2) 与聊天机器人的开放对话可以帮助作家探索他们的角色并获得灵感，这是通过考虑定义的角色属性的语言模型实现的。我们的用户研究揭示了特别是对于角色创建的早期阶段的好处，以及由于对话能力有限而带来的挑战。最后我们总结了经验教训和对未来工作的想法。

AI.tldr: 开发CharacterChat聊天机器人，通过对话和渐进式展示辅助作家创建虚构角色。
AI.motivation: 传统角色创建过程复杂，作家需要工具辅助，以提高效率和角色深度。
AI.method: 采用用户中心设计，结合引导性问题和语言模型，通过迭代开发形成原型系统。
AI.result: 用户研究显示系统在角色创建初期有效，但受限于对话能力。
AI.conclusion: 系统有效辅助角色创建，未来需提升对话能力和个性化。

title: Learning from Pseudo Lesion: A Self-supervised Framework for COVID-19 Diagnosis
title_zh: 从假性病变中学习：COVID-19 诊断的自我监督框架
abstract: The Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) has rapidly spread all over the world since its first report in December 2019 and thoracic computed tomography (CT) has become one of the main tools for its diagnosis. In recent years, deep learning-based approaches have shown impressive performance in myriad image recognition tasks. However, they usually require a large number of annotated data for training. Inspired by Ground Glass Opacity (GGO), a common finding in COIVD-19 patient's CT scans, we proposed in this paper a novel self-supervised pretraining method based on pseudo lesions generation and restoration for COVID-19 diagnosis. We used Perlin noise, a gradient noise based mathematical model, to generate lesion-like patterns, which were then randomly pasted to the lung regions of normal CT images to generate pseudo COVID-19 images. The pairs of normal and pseudo COVID-19 images were then used to train an encoder-decoder architecture based U-Net for image restoration, which does not require any labelled data. The pretrained encoder was then fine-tuned using labelled data for COVID-19 diagnosis task. Two public COVID-19 diagnosis datasets made up of CT images were employed for evaluation. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrated that the proposed self-supervised learning approach could extract better feature representation for COVID-19 diagnosis and the accuracy of the proposed method outperformed the supervised model pretrained on large scale images by 6.57% and 3.03% on SARS-CoV-2 dataset and Jinan COVID-19 dataset, respectively.
summary: The Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) has rapidly spread all over the world since its first report in December 2019 and thoracic computed tomography (CT) has become one of the main tools for its diagnosis. In recent years, deep learning-based approaches have shown impressive performance in myriad image recognition tasks. However, they usually require a large number of annotated data for training. Inspired by Ground Glass Opacity (GGO), a common finding in COIVD-19 patient's CT scans, we proposed in this paper a novel self-supervised pretraining method based on pseudo lesions generation and restoration for COVID-19 diagnosis. We used Perlin noise, a gradient noise based mathematical model, to generate lesion-like patterns, which were then randomly pasted to the lung regions of normal CT images to generate pseudo COVID-19 images. The pairs of normal and pseudo COVID-19 images were then used to train an encoder-decoder architecture based U-Net for image restoration, which does not require any labelled data. The pretrained encoder was then fine-tuned using labelled data for COVID-19 diagnosis task. Two public COVID-19 diagnosis datasets made up of CT images were employed for evaluation. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrated that the proposed self-supervised learning approach could extract better feature representation for COVID-19 diagnosis and the accuracy of the proposed method outperformed the supervised model pretrained on large scale images by 6.57% and 3.03% on SARS-CoV-2 dataset and Jinan COVID-19 dataset, respectively.
abstract_zh: 自2019年12月首次报告以来，2019冠状病毒病（COVID-19）已在世界各地迅速传播，胸部计算机断层扫描（CT）已成为其诊断的主要工具之一。近年来，基于深度学习的方法在无数图像识别任务中表现出了令人印象深刻的性能。然而，它们通常需要大量带注释的数据进行训练。受到 COIVD-19 患者 CT 扫描中常见的毛玻璃混浊 (GGO) 的启发，我们在本文中提出了一种基于伪病灶生成和恢复的新型自监督预训练方法，用于 COVID-19 诊断。我们使用 Perlin 噪声（一种基于梯度噪声的数学模型）来生成病变样图案，然后将其随机粘贴到正常 CT 图像的肺部区域以生成伪 COVID-19 图像。然后，使用这对正常和伪 COVID-19 图像来训练基于 U-Net 的编码器-解码器架构以进行图像恢复，这不需要任何标记数据。然后使用用于 COVID-19 诊断任务的标记数据对预训练的编码器进行微调。采用两个由 CT 图像组成的公共 COVID-19 诊断数据集进行评估。综合实验结果表明，所提出的自监督学习方法可以为 COVID-19 诊断提取更好的特征表示，并且该方法的准确性在 SARS-CoV-2 数据集和济南 COVID-19 数据集上分别优于在大规模图像上预训练的监督模型 6.57% 和 3.03%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于伪病灶生成与修复的自监督预训练方法，用于改进COVID-19的CT影像诊断。
AI.motivation: 深度学习方法通常需要大量标注数据，而COVID-19的CT影像标注数据稀缺。受COVID-19患者CT影像中常见的磨玻璃影（GGO）启发，提出无需标注数据的自监督学习框架。
AI.method: 利用Perlin噪声生成伪病灶图案并粘贴到正常CT影像中，构建正常与伪COVID-19影像对，训练U-Net编码-解码器进行图像修复，再通过标注数据微调预训练编码器。
AI.result: 在SARS-CoV-2和济南COVID-19数据集上，该方法分别比基于大规模图像预训练的监督模型准确率提升6.57%和3.03%。
AI.conclusion: 自监督学习方法能有效提取COVID-19诊断的特征表示，且在无标注数据情况下仍可实现优于传统监督模型的诊断性能。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于伪病灶生成与修复的自监督预训练方法，用于改进COVID-19的CT影像诊断。

search_text: Learning from Pseudo Lesion: A Self-supervised Framework for COVID-19 Diagnosis 从假性病变中学习：COVID-19 诊断的自我监督框架 The Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) has rapidly spread all over the world since its first report in December 2019 and thoracic computed tomography (CT) has become one of the main tools for its diagnosis. In recent years, deep learning-based approaches have shown impressive performance in myriad image recognition tasks. However, they usually require a large number of annotated data for training. Inspired by Ground Glass Opacity (GGO), a common finding in COIVD-19 patient's CT scans, we proposed in this paper a novel self-supervised pretraining method based on pseudo lesions generation and restoration for COVID-19 diagnosis. We used Perlin noise, a gradient noise based mathematical model, to generate lesion-like patterns, which were then randomly pasted to the lung regions of normal CT images to generate pseudo COVID-19 images. The pairs of normal and pseudo COVID-19 images were then used to train an encoder-decoder architecture based U-Net for image restoration, which does not require any labelled data. The pretrained encoder was then fine-tuned using labelled data for COVID-19 diagnosis task. Two public COVID-19 diagnosis datasets made up of CT images were employed for evaluation. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrated that the proposed self-supervised learning approach could extract better feature representation for COVID-19 diagnosis and the accuracy of the proposed method outperformed the supervised model pretrained on large scale images by 6.57% and 3.03% on SARS-CoV-2 dataset and Jinan COVID-19 dataset, respectively. 自2019年12月首次报告以来，2019冠状病毒病（COVID-19）已在世界各地迅速传播，胸部计算机断层扫描（CT）已成为其诊断的主要工具之一。近年来，基于深度学习的方法在无数图像识别任务中表现出了令人印象深刻的性能。然而，它们通常需要大量带注释的数据进行训练。受到 COIVD-19 患者 CT 扫描中常见的毛玻璃混浊 (GGO) 的启发，我们在本文中提出了一种基于伪病灶生成和恢复的新型自监督预训练方法，用于 COVID-19 诊断。我们使用 Perlin 噪声（一种基于梯度噪声的数学模型）来生成病变样图案，然后将其随机粘贴到正常 CT 图像的肺部区域以生成伪 COVID-19 图像。然后，使用这对正常和伪 COVID-19 图像来训练基于 U-Net 的编码器-解码器架构以进行图像恢复，这不需要任何标记数据。然后使用用于 COVID-19 诊断任务的标记数据对预训练的编码器进行微调。采用两个由 CT 图像组成的公共 COVID-19 诊断数据集进行评估。综合实验结果表明，所提出的自监督学习方法可以为 COVID-19 诊断提取更好的特征表示，并且该方法的准确性在 SARS-CoV-2 数据集和济南 COVID-19 数据集上分别优于在大规模图像上预训练的监督模型 6.57% 和 3.03%。 本文提出了一种基于伪病灶生成与修复的自监督预训练方法，用于改进COVID-19的CT影像诊断。 Zhongliang Li,Zhihao Jin,Xuechen Li,Linlin Shen eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Mats L. Richter,Julius Schöning,Anna Wiedenroth,Ulf Krumnack
title: Should You Go Deeper? Optimizing Convolutional Neural Network Architectures without Training by Receptive Field Analysis
title_zh: 你应该更深入吗？通过感受野分析优化无需训练的卷积神经网络架构
abstract: When optimizing convolutional neural networks (CNN) for a specific image-based task, specialists commonly overshoot the number of convolutional layers in their designs. By implication, these CNNs are unnecessarily resource intensive to train and deploy, with diminishing beneficial effects on the predictive performance. The features a convolutional layer can process are strictly limited by its receptive field. By layer-wise analyzing the size of the receptive fields, we can reliably predict sequences of layers that will not contribute qualitatively to the test accuracy in the given CNN architecture. Based on this analysis, we propose design strategies based on a so-called border layer. This layer allows to identify unproductive convolutional layers and hence to resolve these inefficiencies, optimize the explainability and the computational performance of CNNs. Since neither the strategies nor the analysis requires training of the actual model, these insights allow for a very efficient design process of CNN architectures, which might be automated in the future.
summary: When optimizing convolutional neural networks (CNN) for a specific image-based task, specialists commonly overshoot the number of convolutional layers in their designs. By implication, these CNNs are unnecessarily resource intensive to train and deploy, with diminishing beneficial effects on the predictive performance. The features a convolutional layer can process are strictly limited by its receptive field. By layer-wise analyzing the size of the receptive fields, we can reliably predict sequences of layers that will not contribute qualitatively to the test accuracy in the given CNN architecture. Based on this analysis, we propose design strategies based on a so-called border layer. This layer allows to identify unproductive convolutional layers and hence to resolve these inefficiencies, optimize the explainability and the computational performance of CNNs. Since neither the strategies nor the analysis requires training of the actual model, these insights allow for a very efficient design process of CNN architectures, which might be automated in the future.
abstract_zh: 在针对特定的基于图像的任务优化卷积神经网络 (CNN) 时，专家通常会超出设计中的卷积层数量。言下之意是，这些 CNN 的训练和部署不必要地耗费资源，并且对预测性能的有益影响逐渐减弱。卷积层可以处理的特征受到其感受野的严格限制。通过逐层分析感受野的大小，我们可以可靠地预测不会对给定 CNN 架构中的测试精度做出定性贡献的层序列。基于此分析，我们提出了基于所谓的边界层的设计策略。该层可以识别无效的卷积层，从而解决这些低效问题，优化 CNN 的可解释性和计算性能。由于策略和分析都不需要对实际模型进行训练，因此这些见解可以实现非常高效的 CNN 架构设计过程，并且在未来可能会实现自动化。

AI.tldr: 通过感受野分析优化CNN架构，无需训练即可提高效率。
AI.motivation: 现有CNN设计常因层数过多导致资源浪费，需优化以提高效率。
AI.method: 通过逐层分析感受野大小，结合边界层识别无贡献层，优化网络结构。
AI.result: 有效识别冗余层，提升计算效率和模型可解释性，无需训练。
AI.conclusion: 无需训练即可优化CNN设计，提升效率，未来可自动化。

title: A Fokker-Planck Solver to Model MTJ Stochasticity
title_zh: 用于模拟 MTJ 随机性的 Fokker-Planck 求解器
abstract: Magnetic Tunnel Junctions (MTJs) constitute the novel memory element in STT-MRAM, which is ramping to production at major foundries as an eFlash replacement. MTJ switching exhibits a stochastic behavior due to thermal fluctuations, which is modeled by s-LLGS and Fokker-Planck (FP) equations. This work implements and benchmarks Finite Volume Method (FVM) and analytical solvers for the FP equation. To deploy an MTJ model for circuit design, it must be calibrated against silicon data. To address this challenge, this work presents a regression scheme to fit MTJ parameters to a given set of measured current, switching time and error rate data points, yielding a silicon-calibrated model suitable for MRAM macro transient simulation.
summary: Magnetic Tunnel Junctions (MTJs) constitute the novel memory element in STT-MRAM, which is ramping to production at major foundries as an eFlash replacement. MTJ switching exhibits a stochastic behavior due to thermal fluctuations, which is modeled by s-LLGS and Fokker-Planck (FP) equations. This work implements and benchmarks Finite Volume Method (FVM) and analytical solvers for the FP equation. To deploy an MTJ model for circuit design, it must be calibrated against silicon data. To address this challenge, this work presents a regression scheme to fit MTJ parameters to a given set of measured current, switching time and error rate data points, yielding a silicon-calibrated model suitable for MRAM macro transient simulation.
abstract_zh: 磁隧道结 (MTJ) 构成了 STT-MRAM 中的新型存储元件，作为 eFlash 的替代品，该元件正在主要代工厂投入生产。 MTJ 开关由于热波动而表现出随机行为，该行为通过 s-LLGS 和 Fokker-Planck (FP) 方程进行建模。这项工作实现了 FP 方程的有限体积法 (FVM) 和分析求解器并对其进行了基准测试。要部署 MTJ 模型进行电路设计，必须根据硅数据对其进行校准。为了应对这一挑战，这项工作提出了一种回归方案，将 MTJ 参数拟合到一组给定的测量电流、开关时间和错误率数据点，从而生成适合 MRAM 宏观瞬态仿真的硅校准模型。

AI.tldr: 开发并验证用于模拟MTJ随机性的Fokker-Planck方程求解器，并校准MTJ模型以适应电路设计需求。
AI.motivation: STT-MRAM作为eFlash替代品需要精确建模其随机性，以电路设计为目的需校准模型参数。
AI.method: 采用有限体积法（FVM）和解析求解器求解FP方程，并利用回归方法拟合参数以校准模型。
AI.result: 获得硅校准的MTJ模型，适用于MRAM宏的瞬态仿真。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了MTJ模型的准确性，为MRAM电路设计提供可靠支持。

title: Estimating the Robustness of Classification Models by the Structure of the Learned Feature-Space
title_zh: 通过学习的特征空间的结构估计分类模型的鲁棒性
abstract: Over the last decade, the development of deep image classification networks has mostly been driven by the search for the best performance in terms of classification accuracy on standardized benchmarks like ImageNet. More recently, this focus has been expanded by the notion of model robustness, \ie the generalization abilities of models towards previously unseen changes in the data distribution. While new benchmarks, like ImageNet-C, have been introduced to measure robustness properties, we argue that fixed testsets are only able to capture a small portion of possible data variations and are thus limited and prone to generate new overfitted solutions. To overcome these drawbacks, we suggest to estimate the robustness of a model directly from the structure of its learned feature-space. We introduce robustness indicators which are obtained via unsupervised clustering of latent representations from a trained classifier and show very high correlations to the model performance on corrupted test data.
summary: Over the last decade, the development of deep image classification networks has mostly been driven by the search for the best performance in terms of classification accuracy on standardized benchmarks like ImageNet. More recently, this focus has been expanded by the notion of model robustness, \ie the generalization abilities of models towards previously unseen changes in the data distribution. While new benchmarks, like ImageNet-C, have been introduced to measure robustness properties, we argue that fixed testsets are only able to capture a small portion of possible data variations and are thus limited and prone to generate new overfitted solutions. To overcome these drawbacks, we suggest to estimate the robustness of a model directly from the structure of its learned feature-space. We introduce robustness indicators which are obtained via unsupervised clustering of latent representations from a trained classifier and show very high correlations to the model performance on corrupted test data.
abstract_zh: 在过去的十年中，深度图像分类网络的发展主要是通过在 ImageNet 等标准化基准上寻求分类精度方面的最佳性能来推动的。最近，模型鲁棒性的概念扩展了这一焦点，即模型对数据分布中以前未见过的变化的泛化能力。虽然已经引入了新的基准（例如 ImageNet-C）来测量鲁棒性属性，但我们认为固定测试集​​只能捕获一小部分可能的数据变化，因此受到限制并且容易生成新的过度拟合解决方案。为了克服这些缺点，我们建议直接根据模型学习到的特征空间的结构来估计模型的鲁棒性。我们引入了鲁棒性指标，这些指标是通过对经过训练的分类器的潜在表示进行无监督聚类而获得的，并且在损坏的测试数据上显示出与模型性能的非常高的相关性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过分析分类模型学习到的特征空间结构来评估模型鲁棒性的方法，无需依赖传统固定测试集。
AI.motivation: 传统鲁棒性评估方法依赖固定测试集（如ImageNet-C），但其仅能捕捉有限的数据变化模式，易导致过拟合且无法全面反映模型泛化能力。
AI.method: 通过无监督聚类分析训练分类器的潜在特征表示，提取反映特征空间结构的鲁棒性指标。
AI.result: 所提指标与模型在损坏数据上的性能表现呈现高度相关性，验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 直接分析特征空间结构可更全面评估模型鲁棒性，克服传统测试集的局限性。

search_text: Estimating the Robustness of Classification Models by the Structure of the Learned Feature-Space 通过学习的特征空间的结构估计分类模型的鲁棒性 Over the last decade, the development of deep image classification networks has mostly been driven by the search for the best performance in terms of classification accuracy on standardized benchmarks like ImageNet. More recently, this focus has been expanded by the notion of model robustness, \ie the generalization abilities of models towards previously unseen changes in the data distribution. While new benchmarks, like ImageNet-C, have been introduced to measure robustness properties, we argue that fixed testsets are only able to capture a small portion of possible data variations and are thus limited and prone to generate new overfitted solutions. To overcome these drawbacks, we suggest to estimate the robustness of a model directly from the structure of its learned feature-space. We introduce robustness indicators which are obtained via unsupervised clustering of latent representations from a trained classifier and show very high correlations to the model performance on corrupted test data. 在过去的十年中，深度图像分类网络的发展主要是通过在 ImageNet 等标准化基准上寻求分类精度方面的最佳性能来推动的。最近，模型鲁棒性的概念扩展了这一焦点，即模型对数据分布中以前未见过的变化的泛化能力。虽然已经引入了新的基准（例如 ImageNet-C）来测量鲁棒性属性，但我们认为固定测试集​​只能捕获一小部分可能的数据变化，因此受到限制并且容易生成新的过度拟合解决方案。为了克服这些缺点，我们建议直接根据模型学习到的特征空间的结构来估计模型的鲁棒性。我们引入了鲁棒性指标，这些指标是通过对经过训练的分类器的潜在表示进行无监督聚类而获得的，并且在损坏的测试数据上显示出与模型性能的非常高的相关性。 本文提出一种通过分析分类模型学习到的特征空间结构来评估模型鲁棒性的方法，无需依赖传统固定测试集。 Kalun Ho,Franz-Josef Pfreundt,Janis Keuper,Margret Keuper cs.CV

title: 3D human tongue reconstruction from single "in-the-wild" images
title_zh: 从单个“野外”图像重建 3D 人类舌头
abstract: 3D face reconstruction from a single image is a task that has garnered increased interest in the Computer Vision community, especially due to its broad use in a number of applications such as realistic 3D avatar creation, pose invariant face recognition and face hallucination. Since the introduction of the 3D Morphable Model in the late 90's, we witnessed an explosion of research aiming at particularly tackling this task. Nevertheless, despite the increasing level of detail in the 3D face reconstructions from single images mainly attributed to deep learning advances, finer and highly deformable components of the face such as the tongue are still absent from all 3D face models in the literature, although being very important for the realness of the 3D avatar representations. In this work we present the first, to the best of our knowledge, end-to-end trainable pipeline that accurately reconstructs the 3D face together with the tongue. Moreover, we make this pipeline robust in "in-the-wild" images by introducing a novel GAN method tailored for 3D tongue surface generation. Finally, we make publicly available to the community the first diverse tongue dataset, consisting of 1,800 raw scans of 700 individuals varying in gender, age, and ethnicity backgrounds. As we demonstrate in an extensive series of quantitative as well as qualitative experiments, our model proves to be robust and realistically captures the 3D tongue structure, even in adverse "in-the-wild" conditions.
summary: 3D face reconstruction from a single image is a task that has garnered increased interest in the Computer Vision community, especially due to its broad use in a number of applications such as realistic 3D avatar creation, pose invariant face recognition and face hallucination. Since the introduction of the 3D Morphable Model in the late 90's, we witnessed an explosion of research aiming at particularly tackling this task. Nevertheless, despite the increasing level of detail in the 3D face reconstructions from single images mainly attributed to deep learning advances, finer and highly deformable components of the face such as the tongue are still absent from all 3D face models in the literature, although being very important for the realness of the 3D avatar representations. In this work we present the first, to the best of our knowledge, end-to-end trainable pipeline that accurately reconstructs the 3D face together with the tongue. Moreover, we make this pipeline robust in "in-the-wild" images by introducing a novel GAN method tailored for 3D tongue surface generation. Finally, we make publicly available to the community the first diverse tongue dataset, consisting of 1,800 raw scans of 700 individuals varying in gender, age, and ethnicity backgrounds. As we demonstrate in an extensive series of quantitative as well as qualitative experiments, our model proves to be robust and realistically captures the 3D tongue structure, even in adverse "in-the-wild" conditions.
abstract_zh: 从单个图像进行 3D 人脸重建是一项在计算机视觉社区中引起越来越多兴趣的任务，特别是由于其在许多应用中的广泛使用，例如逼真的 3D 头像创建、姿势不变的人脸识别和人脸幻觉。自从 90 年代末引入 3D Morphable 模型以来，我们目睹了专门针对这一任务的研究激增。然而，尽管主要归因于深度学习的进步，单图像 3D 面部重建的细节水平不断提高，但文献中的所有 3D 面部模型中仍然缺少更精细且高度可变形的面部组件（例如舌头），尽管这对于 3D 头像表示的真实性非常重要。在这项工作中，据我们所知，我们提出了第一个端到端可训练管道，可以准确地重建 3D 面部和舌头。此外，我们通过引入一种专为 3D 舌头表面生成量身定制的新型 GAN 方法，使该流程在“野外”图像中具有鲁棒性。最后，我们向社区公开提供第一个多样化的舌头数据集，其中包含 700 名不同性别、年龄和种族背景的个体的 1,800 份原始扫描数据。正如我们在一系列广泛的定量和定性实验中所证明的那样，我们的模型被证明是稳健的，并且即使在不利的“野外”条件下也能真实地捕获 3D 舌头结构。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种从单张真实场景图像中重建3D人脸及舌头的端到端方法。
AI.motivation: 现有3D面部重建模型缺乏舌头等细小可变形部件，影响3D模型真实感，而舌头对表情和语音至关重要。
AI.method: 设计端到端可训练流水线，结合定制化GAN生成3D舌头表面，并发布包含1800个扫描样本的多民族舌头数据集。
AI.result: 模型在复杂野外条件下能鲁棒重建舌头结构，定量与定性实验验证了重建结果的真实性。
AI.conclusion: 首次实现3D人脸与舌头联合重建，推动了生物特征建模领域的发展。

title: Behavior Mimics Distribution: Combining Individual and Group Behaviors for Federated Learning
title_zh: 行为模仿分布：结合个人和群体行为进行联邦学习
comment: This paper has been accepted by International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence (IJCAI) 2021
comments: This paper has been accepted by International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence (IJCAI) 2021
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has become an active and promising distributed machine learning paradigm. As a result of statistical heterogeneity, recent studies clearly show that the performance of popular FL methods (e.g., FedAvg) deteriorates dramatically due to the client drift caused by local updates. This paper proposes a novel Federated Learning algorithm (called IGFL), which leverages both Individual and Group behaviors to mimic distribution, thereby improving the ability to deal with heterogeneity. Unlike existing FL methods, our IGFL can be applied to both client and server optimization. As a by-product, we propose a new attention-based federated learning in the server optimization of IGFL. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first time to incorporate attention mechanisms into federated optimization. We conduct extensive experiments and show that IGFL can significantly improve the performance of existing federated learning methods. Especially when the distributions of data among individuals are diverse, IGFL can improve the classification accuracy by about 13% compared with prior baselines.
summary: Federated Learning (FL) has become an active and promising distributed machine learning paradigm. As a result of statistical heterogeneity, recent studies clearly show that the performance of popular FL methods (e.g., FedAvg) deteriorates dramatically due to the client drift caused by local updates. This paper proposes a novel Federated Learning algorithm (called IGFL), which leverages both Individual and Group behaviors to mimic distribution, thereby improving the ability to deal with heterogeneity. Unlike existing FL methods, our IGFL can be applied to both client and server optimization. As a by-product, we propose a new attention-based federated learning in the server optimization of IGFL. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first time to incorporate attention mechanisms into federated optimization. We conduct extensive experiments and show that IGFL can significantly improve the performance of existing federated learning methods. Especially when the distributions of data among individuals are diverse, IGFL can improve the classification accuracy by about 13% compared with prior baselines.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习（FL）已成为一种活跃且有前途的分布式机器学习范例。 As a result of statistical heterogeneity, recent studies clearly show that the performance of popular FL methods (e.g., FedAvg) deteriorates dramatically due to the client drift caused by local updates. This paper proposes a novel Federated Learning algorithm (called IGFL), which leverages both Individual and Group behaviors to mimic distribution, thereby improving the ability to deal with heterogeneity.与现有的 FL 方法不同，我们的 IGFL 可以应用于客户端和服务器优化。作为副产品，我们在 IGFL 的服务器优化中提出了一种新的基于注意力的联邦学习。据我们所知，这是第一次将注意力机制纳入联邦优化中。我们进行了大量的实验，结果表明 IGFL 可以显着提高现有联邦学习方法的性能。 Especially when the distributions of data among individuals are diverse, IGFL can improve the classification accuracy by about 13% compared with prior baselines.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的联邦学习算法IGFL，通过结合个体和群体行为来模拟数据分布，以解决联邦学习中因统计异质性导致的客户端漂移问题。
AI.motivation: 现有的联邦学习方法（如FedAvg）在面对数据分布异质性时性能显著下降，客户端漂移问题亟需改进。
AI.method: 提出IGFL算法，结合个体和群体行为模拟分布，并在服务器优化中引入注意力机制，首次将注意力机制应用于联邦优化。
AI.result: 实验表明，IGFL在数据分布多样化场景下分类准确率相比现有方法提升约13%，显著优于基线方法。
AI.conclusion: IGFL通过融合个体与群体行为及注意力机制，有效缓解了联邦学习中的异质性问题，为联邦优化提供了新思路。

We conduct extensive experiments and show that IGFL can significantly improve the performance of existing federated learning methods. Especially when the distributions of data among individuals are diverse, IGFL can improve the classification accuracy by about 13% compared with prior baselines. 联邦学习（FL）已成为一种活跃且有前途的分布式机器学习范例。 As a result of statistical heterogeneity, recent studies clearly show that the performance of popular FL methods (e.g., FedAvg) deteriorates dramatically due to the client drift caused by local updates. This paper proposes a novel Federated Learning algorithm (called IGFL), which leverages both Individual and Group behaviors to mimic distribution, thereby improving the ability to deal with heterogeneity.与现有的 FL 方法不同，我们的 IGFL 可以应用于客户端和服务器优化。作为副产品，我们在 IGFL 的服务器优化中提出了一种新的基于注意力的联邦学习。据我们所知，这是第一次将注意力机制纳入联邦优化中。我们进行了大量的实验，结果表明 IGFL 可以显着提高现有联邦学习方法的性能。 Especially when the distributions of data among individuals are diverse, IGFL can improve the classification accuracy by about 13% compared with prior baselines. 本文提出了一种新的联邦学习算法IGFL，通过结合个体和群体行为来模拟数据分布，以解决联邦学习中因统计异质性导致的客户端漂移问题。 Hua Huang,Fanhua Shang,Yuanyuan Liu,Hongying Liu cs.LG,cs.CV,cs.DC

authors_text: Davide Bilò,Gianlorenzo D'Angelo,Luciano Gualà,Stefano Leucci,Guido Proietti,Mirko Rossi
title: Finding single-source shortest $p$-disjoint paths: fast computation and sparse preservers
title_zh: 寻找单源最短$p$不相交路径：快速计算和稀疏保护器
abstract: Let $G$ be a directed graph with $n$ vertices, $m$ edges, and non-negative edge costs. Given $G$, a fixed source vertex $s$, and a positive integer $p$, we consider the problem of computing, for each vertex $t\neq s$, $p$ edge-disjoint paths of minimum total cost from $s$ to $t$ in $G$. Suurballe and Tarjan~[Networks, 1984] solved the above problem for $p=2$ by designing a $O(m+n\log n)$ time algorithm which also computes a sparse \emph{single-source $2$-multipath preserver}, i.e., a subgraph containing $2$ edge-disjoint paths of minimum total cost from $s$ to every other vertex of $G$. The case $p \geq 3$ was left as an open problem. We study the general problem ($p\geq 2$) and prove that any graph admits a sparse single-source $p$-multipath preserver with $p(n-1)$ edges. This size is optimal since the in-degree of each non-root vertex $v$ must be at least $p$. Moreover, we design an algorithm that requires $O(pn^2 (p + \log n))$ time to compute both $p$ edge-disjoint paths of minimum total cost from the source to all other vertices and an optimal-size single-source $p$-multipath preserver. The running time of our algorithm outperforms that of a natural approach that solves $n-1$ single-pair instances using the well-known \emph{successive shortest paths} algorithm by a factor of $Θ(\frac{m}{np})$ and is asymptotically near optimal if $p=O(1)$ and $m=Θ(n^2)$. Our results extend naturally to the case of $p$ vertex-disjoint paths.
summary: Let $G$ be a directed graph with $n$ vertices, $m$ edges, and non-negative edge costs. Given $G$, a fixed source vertex $s$, and a positive integer $p$, we consider the problem of computing, for each vertex $t\neq s$, $p$ edge-disjoint paths of minimum total cost from $s$ to $t$ in $G$. Suurballe and Tarjan~[Networks, 1984] solved the above problem for $p=2$ by designing a $O(m+n\log n)$ time algorithm which also computes a sparse \emph{single-source $2$-multipath preserver}, i.e., a subgraph containing $2$ edge-disjoint paths of minimum total cost from $s$ to every other vertex of $G$. The case $p \geq 3$ was left as an open problem. We study the general problem ($p\geq 2$) and prove that any graph admits a sparse single-source $p$-multipath preserver with $p(n-1)$ edges. This size is optimal since the in-degree of each non-root vertex $v$ must be at least $p$. Moreover, we design an algorithm that requires $O(pn^2 (p + \log n))$ time to compute both $p$ edge-disjoint paths of minimum total cost from the source to all other vertices and an optimal-size single-source $p$-multipath preserver. The running time of our algorithm outperforms that of a natural approach that solves $n-1$ single-pair instances using the well-known \emph{successive shortest paths} algorithm by a factor of $Θ(\frac{m}{np})$ and is asymptotically near optimal if $p=O(1)$ and $m=Θ(n^2)$. Our results extend naturally to the case of $p$ vertex-disjoint paths.
abstract_zh: 令 $G$ 为具有 $n$ 个顶点、$m$ 个边和非负边成本的有向图。给定$G$、固定源顶点$s$和正整数$p$，我们考虑计算问题，对于每个顶点$t\neq s$，计算$G$中从$s$到$t$的最小总成本的$p$边不相交路径。 Suurballe 和 Tarjan~[Networks, 1984] 通过设计 $O(m+n\log n)$ 时间算法解决了 $p=2$ 的上述问题，该算法还计算稀疏 \emph{单源 $2$-多路径保护器}，即包含从 $s$ 到 $G$ 的每个其他顶点的总成本最小的 $2$ 边不相交路径的子图。 $p \geq 3$ 的情况作为一个悬而未决的问题。我们研究一般问题 ($p\geq 2$) 并证明任何图都承认具有 $p(n-1)$ 边的稀疏单源 $p$-多路径保护器。此大小是最佳的，因为每个非根顶点 $v$ 的入度必须至少为 $p$。此外，我们设计了一种算法，需要 $O(pn^2 (p + \log n))$ 时间来计算从源到所有其他顶点的最小总成本的 $p$ 边不相交路径和最佳大小的单源 $p$ 多路径保护器。我们的算法的运行时间比使用众所周知的 \emph{连续最短路径} 算法求解 $n-1$ 个单对实例的自然方法的运行时间高出 $θ(\frac{m}{np})$ 倍，并且在 $p=O(1)$ 和 $m=θ(n^2)$ 的情况下渐近接近最优。我们的结果自然地扩展到 $p$ 顶点不相交路径的情况。

AI.tldr: 本文研究在有向图中为每个顶点计算从固定源点出发的p条边不相交最短路径问题，并设计稀疏的单源p-路径保持子图。
AI.motivation: 现有算法仅解决p=2的情况，而p≥3时未被解决。需要更通用的方法以高效计算多条不相交最短路径并保持子图稀疏性。
AI.method: 提出一种时间复杂度为O(pn²(p+logn))的算法，结合稀疏子图构造技术，证明单源p-路径保持子图的最优边数为p(n-1)。
AI.result: 实现任意图的单源p-路径保持子图构造，边数达到理论下界；算法效率优于传统方法Θ(m/(np))倍，且适用于顶点不相交情形。
AI.conclusion: 解决了p≥2的通用问题，提供近似最优算法及保持子图结构，为网络路由与容错设计提供理论支持。

authors_text: Mengdi Gao,Ximeng Feng,Mufeng Geng,Zhe Jiang,Lei Zhu,Xiangxi Meng,Chuanqing Zhou,Qiushi Ren,Yanye Lu
title: Bayesian Statistics Guided Label Refurbishment Mechanism: Mitigating Label Noise in Medical Image Classification
title_zh: 贝叶斯统计引导标签翻新机制：减轻医学图像分类中的标签噪声
abstract: Purpose: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have been widely applied in medical image classification, benefiting from its powerful mapping capability among medical images. However, these existing deep learning-based methods depend on an enormous amount of carefully labeled images. Meanwhile, noise is inevitably introduced in the labeling process, degrading the performance of models. Hence, it's significant to devise robust training strategies to mitigate label noise in the medical image classification tasks. Methods: In this work, we propose a novel Bayesian statistics guided label refurbishment mechanism (BLRM) for DNNs to prevent overfitting noisy images. BLRM utilizes maximum a posteriori probability (MAP) in the Bayesian statistics and the exponentially time-weighted technique to selectively correct the labels of noisy images. The training images are purified gradually with the training epochs when BLRM is activated, further improving classification performance. Results: Comprehensive experiments on both synthetic noisy images (public OCT & Messidor datasets) and real-world noisy images (ANIMAL-10N) demonstrate that BLRM refurbishes the noisy labels selectively, curbing the adverse effects of noisy data. Also, the anti-noise BLRM integrated with DNNs are effective at different noise ratio and are independent of backbone DNN architectures. In addition, BLRM is superior to state-of-the-art comparative methods of anti-noise. Conclusions: These investigations indicate that the proposed BLRM is well capable of mitigating label noise in medical image classification tasks.
summary: Purpose: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have been widely applied in medical image classification, benefiting from its powerful mapping capability among medical images. However, these existing deep learning-based methods depend on an enormous amount of carefully labeled images. Meanwhile, noise is inevitably introduced in the labeling process, degrading the performance of models. Hence, it's significant to devise robust training strategies to mitigate label noise in the medical image classification tasks. Methods: In this work, we propose a novel Bayesian statistics guided label refurbishment mechanism (BLRM) for DNNs to prevent overfitting noisy images. BLRM utilizes maximum a posteriori probability (MAP) in the Bayesian statistics and the exponentially time-weighted technique to selectively correct the labels of noisy images. The training images are purified gradually with the training epochs when BLRM is activated, further improving classification performance. Results: Comprehensive experiments on both synthetic noisy images (public OCT & Messidor datasets) and real-world noisy images (ANIMAL-10N) demonstrate that BLRM refurbishes the noisy labels selectively, curbing the adverse effects of noisy data. Also, the anti-noise BLRM integrated with DNNs are effective at different noise ratio and are independent of backbone DNN architectures. In addition, BLRM is superior to state-of-the-art comparative methods of anti-noise. Conclusions: These investigations indicate that the proposed BLRM is well capable of mitigating label noise in medical image classification tasks.
abstract_zh: 目的：深度神经网络（DNN）受益于其强大的医学图像之间的映射能力，在医学图像分类中得到了广泛的应用。然而，这些现有的基于深度学习的方法依赖于大量仔细标记的图像。同时，在标记过程中不可避免地引入噪声，降低模型的性能。因此，设计强大的训练策略来减轻医学图像分类任务中的标签噪声非常重要。方法：在这项工作中，我们为 DNN 提出了一种新颖的贝叶斯统计引导标签翻新机制（BLRM），以防止过度拟合噪声图像。 BLRM利用贝叶斯统计中的最大后验概率（MAP）和指数时间加权技术来选择性地校正噪声图像的标签。当 BLRM 激活时，训练图像随着训练周期逐渐纯化，进一步提高分类性能。结果：对合成噪声图像（公共 OCT 和 Messidor 数据集）和真实世界噪声图像 (ANIMAL-10N) 的综合实验表明，BLRM 选择性地翻新噪声标签，抑制噪声数据的不利影响。此外，与 DNN 集成的抗噪声 BLRM 在不同噪声比下均有效，并且独立于骨干 DNN 架构。此外，BLRM 优于最先进的抗噪声比较方法。结论：这些研究表明，所提出的 BLRM 能够很好地减轻医学图像分类任务中的标签噪声。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于贝叶斯统计的标签修复机制（BLRM），用于减少医学图像分类中的标签噪声影响。
AI.motivation: 深度学习方法依赖大量精确标注的医学图像，但标注过程不可避免地引入噪声，导致模型性能下降，需开发鲁棒的训练策略应对标签噪声。
AI.method: 通过贝叶斯统计中的最大后验概率（MAP）与指数时间加权技术，选择性修正噪声标签，随训练轮次逐步净化训练图像。
AI.result: 在合成噪声数据集（OCT/Messidor）和真实噪声数据集（ANIMAL-10N）实验表明，BLRM能有效抑制噪声影响，且对不同噪声比例和网络结构均具鲁棒性，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: BLRM机制能有效缓解医学图像分类任务中的标签噪声问题，提升模型性能。

Also, the anti-noise BLRM integrated with DNNs are effective at different noise ratio and are independent of backbone DNN architectures. Conclusions: These investigations indicate that the proposed BLRM is well capable of mitigating label noise in medical image classification tasks. 目的：深度神经网络（DNN）受益于其强大的医学图像之间的映射能力，在医学图像分类中得到了广泛的应用。然而，这些现有的基于深度学习的方法依赖于大量仔细标记的图像。同时，在标记过程中不可避免地引入噪声，降低模型的性能。因此，设计强大的训练策略来减轻医学图像分类任务中的标签噪声非常重要。方法：在这项工作中，我们为 DNN 提出了一种新颖的贝叶斯统计引导标签翻新机制（BLRM），以防止过度拟合噪声图像。 BLRM利用贝叶斯统计中的最大后验概率（MAP）和指数时间加权技术来选择性地校正噪声图像的标签。当 BLRM 激活时，训练图像随着训练周期逐渐纯化，进一步提高分类性能。结果：对合成噪声图像（公共 OCT 和 Messidor 数据集）和真实世界噪声图像 (ANIMAL-10N) 的综合实验表明，BLRM 选择性地翻新噪声标签，抑制噪声数据的不利影响。此外，与 DNN 集成的抗噪声 BLRM 在不同噪声比下均有效，并且独立于骨干 DNN 架构。此外，BLRM 优于最先进的抗噪声比较方法。结论：这些研究表明，所提出的 BLRM 能够很好地减轻医学图像分类任务中的标签噪声。 本文提出一种基于贝叶斯统计的标签修复机制（BLRM），用于减少医学图像分类中的标签噪声影响。 Mengdi Gao,Ximeng Feng,Mufeng Geng,Zhe Jiang,Lei Zhu,Xiangxi Meng,Chuanqing Zhou,Qiushi Ren,Yanye Lu cs.CV,cs.AI

title: Deep unsupervised 3D human body reconstruction from a sparse set of landmarks
title_zh: 从一组稀疏地标进行深度无监督 3D 人体重建
abstract: In this paper we propose the first deep unsupervised approach in human body reconstruction to estimate body surface from a sparse set of landmarks, so called DeepMurf. We apply a denoising autoencoder to estimate missing landmarks. Then we apply an attention model to estimate body joints from landmarks. Finally, a cascading network is applied to regress parameters of a statistical generative model that reconstructs body. Our set of proposed loss functions allows us to train the network in an unsupervised way. Results on four public datasets show that our approach accurately reconstructs the human body from real world mocap data.
summary: In this paper we propose the first deep unsupervised approach in human body reconstruction to estimate body surface from a sparse set of landmarks, so called DeepMurf. We apply a denoising autoencoder to estimate missing landmarks. Then we apply an attention model to estimate body joints from landmarks. Finally, a cascading network is applied to regress parameters of a statistical generative model that reconstructs body. Our set of proposed loss functions allows us to train the network in an unsupervised way. Results on four public datasets show that our approach accurately reconstructs the human body from real world mocap data.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了人体重建中第一个深度无监督方法，从一组稀疏的地标中估计身体表面，即 DeepMurf。我们应用去噪自动编码器来估计丢失的地标。然后我们应用注意力模型来根据地标估计身体关节。最后，应用级联网络来回归重建身体的统计生成模型的参数。我们提出的一组损失函数允许我们以无监督的方式训练网络。四个公共数据集的结果表明，我们的方法可以根据现实世界的动作捕捉数据准确地重建人体。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种深度无监督方法DeepMurf，从稀疏地标点重建3D人体表面。
AI.motivation: 传统人体重建方法依赖大量标注数据，而本文旨在通过无监督学习减少对标注数据的依赖。
AI.method: 采用去噪自编码器估计缺失地标，利用注意力模型预测关节位置，通过级联网络回归统计生成模型参数，并设计无监督损失函数进行训练。
AI.result: 在四个公开数据集上验证，方法能准确重建真实世界动作捕捉数据中的人体模型。
AI.conclusion: DeepMurf证明了无监督学习在稀疏地标人体重建中的有效性，为数据标注受限场景提供新解决方案。

title: #StayHome Playing LoL -- Analyzing Players' Activity and Social Bonds in League of Legends During Covid-19 Lockdowns
title_zh: #StayHome Playing LoL——分析 Covid-19 封锁期间《英雄联盟》玩家的活动和社交关系
comment: accepted for publication in Foundations of Digital Games (FDG) 2021
comments: accepted for publication in Foundations of Digital Games (FDG) 2021
abstract: Humans are social beings. It is therefore not surprising that the social distancing and movement restrictions associated with the Covid-19 pandemic had severe consequences for the well-being of large sections of the population, leading to increased loneliness and related mental diseases. Many people found emotional shelter in online multiplayer games, which have already proven to be great social incubators. While the positive effect games had on individuals has become evident, we are still unaware of how and if games fostered the fundamental need for connectedness that people sought. In other words: how have the social bonds and interaction patterns of players changed with the advent of the pandemic? For this purpose, we analyzed one year of data from an online multiplayer game (League of Legends) to observe the impact of Covid-19 on player assiduity and sociality in three different geographical regions (i.e., Europe, North America, and South Korea). Our results show a strong relationship between the development of Covid-19 restrictions and player activity, together with more robust and recurrent social bonds, especially for players committed to the game. Additionally, players with reinforced social bonds-i.e., people played with similar teammates-were more likely to stay in the game even once the restrictions were lifted.
summary: Humans are social beings. It is therefore not surprising that the social distancing and movement restrictions associated with the Covid-19 pandemic had severe consequences for the well-being of large sections of the population, leading to increased loneliness and related mental diseases. Many people found emotional shelter in online multiplayer games, which have already proven to be great social incubators. While the positive effect games had on individuals has become evident, we are still unaware of how and if games fostered the fundamental need for connectedness that people sought. In other words: how have the social bonds and interaction patterns of players changed with the advent of the pandemic? For this purpose, we analyzed one year of data from an online multiplayer game (League of Legends) to observe the impact of Covid-19 on player assiduity and sociality in three different geographical regions (i.e., Europe, North America, and South Korea). Our results show a strong relationship between the development of Covid-19 restrictions and player activity, together with more robust and recurrent social bonds, especially for players committed to the game. Additionally, players with reinforced social bonds-i.e., people played with similar teammates-were more likely to stay in the game even once the restrictions were lifted.
abstract_zh: 人类是社会生物。因此，与 Covid-19 大流行相关的社交距离和行动限制对大部分人口的福祉造成严重后果，导致孤独感和相关精神疾病的增加，也就不足为奇了。许多人在在线多人游戏中找到了情感庇护所，这些游戏已经被证明是很好的社交孵化器。虽然游戏对个人的积极影响已经显而易见，但我们仍然不知道游戏如何以及是否促进了人们所寻求的联系的基本需求。换句话说：随着疫情的到来，玩家的社会纽带和互动模式发生了怎样的变化？为此，我们分析了一款在线多人游戏（《英雄联盟》）一年的数据，以观察 Covid-19 对三个不同地理区域（即欧洲、北美和韩国）玩家的勤勉度和社交性的影响。我们的研究结果表明，Covid-19 限制的发展与玩家活动之间存在密切关系，以及更强大和经常性的社会纽带，特别是对于致力于游戏的玩家而言。此外，即使限制解除，具有强化社会联系的玩家（即与相似队友一起玩的人）也更有可能留在游戏中。

AI.tldr: 分析新冠疫情封锁期间《英雄联盟》玩家的活跃度和社会关系变化
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情导致社交隔离，人们转向在线游戏寻求情感支持，但游戏对社交需求满足的具体影响尚不明确
AI.method: 通过分析《英雄联盟》一年的游戏数据，对比欧洲、北美和韩国三个地区玩家在疫情前后的活跃度及社交模式变化
AI.result: 疫情限制与玩家活跃度呈正相关，长期玩家形成更稳固的社交纽带，且这些玩家在封锁解除后仍保持较高留存率
AI.conclusion: 在线游戏在疫情期间有效维持了玩家社交联系，强化的社交关系成为玩家持续参与的重要因素
