authors_text: Ajil Jalal,Sushrut Karmalkar,Jessica Hoffmann,Alexandros G. Dimakis,Eric Price
title: Fairness for Image Generation with Uncertain Sensitive Attributes
title_zh: 具有不确定敏感属性的图像生成的公平性
abstract: This work tackles the issue of fairness in the context of generative procedures, such as image super-resolution, which entail different definitions from the standard classification setting. Moreover, while traditional group fairness definitions are typically defined with respect to specified protected groups -- camouflaging the fact that these groupings are artificial and carry historical and political motivations -- we emphasize that there are no ground truth identities. For instance, should South and East Asians be viewed as a single group or separate groups? Should we consider one race as a whole or further split by gender? Choosing which groups are valid and who belongs in them is an impossible dilemma and being "fair" with respect to Asians may require being "unfair" with respect to South Asians. This motivates the introduction of definitions that allow algorithms to be \emph{oblivious} to the relevant groupings. We define several intuitive notions of group fairness and study their incompatibilities and trade-offs. We show that the natural extension of demographic parity is strongly dependent on the grouping, and \emph{impossible} to achieve obliviously. On the other hand, the conceptually new definition we introduce, Conditional Proportional Representation, can be achieved obliviously through Posterior Sampling. Our experiments validate our theoretical results and achieve fair image reconstruction using state-of-the-art generative models.
summary: This work tackles the issue of fairness in the context of generative procedures, such as image super-resolution, which entail different definitions from the standard classification setting. Moreover, while traditional group fairness definitions are typically defined with respect to specified protected groups -- camouflaging the fact that these groupings are artificial and carry historical and political motivations -- we emphasize that there are no ground truth identities. For instance, should South and East Asians be viewed as a single group or separate groups? Should we consider one race as a whole or further split by gender? Choosing which groups are valid and who belongs in them is an impossible dilemma and being "fair" with respect to Asians may require being "unfair" with respect to South Asians. This motivates the introduction of definitions that allow algorithms to be \emph{oblivious} to the relevant groupings. We define several intuitive notions of group fairness and study their incompatibilities and trade-offs. We show that the natural extension of demographic parity is strongly dependent on the grouping, and \emph{impossible} to achieve obliviously. On the other hand, the conceptually new definition we introduce, Conditional Proportional Representation, can be achieved obliviously through Posterior Sampling. Our experiments validate our theoretical results and achieve fair image reconstruction using state-of-the-art generative models.
abstract_zh: 这项工作解决了生成过程中的公平性问题，例如图像超分辨率，这需要与标准分类设置不同的定义。此外，虽然传统的群体公平定义通常是针对特定的受保护群体来定义的——掩盖了这些群体是人为的并带有历史和政治动机的事实——但我们强调，不存在真正的身份。例如，南亚人和东亚人应该被视为一个群体还是单独的群体？我们应该将一个种族视为一个整体，还是进一步按性别划分？选择哪些群体是有效的以及谁属于其中是一个不可能的困境，对亚洲人“公平”可能需要对南亚人“不公平”。这促使引入定义，允许算法\emph{忘记}相关的分组。我们定义了群体公平的几个直观概念，并研究了它们的不兼容性和权衡。我们表明，人口均等的自然延伸强烈依赖于分组，并且\emph{不可能}在不知不觉中实现。另一方面，我们引入的概念上的新定义“条件比例表示”可以通过后验采样来实现。我们的实验验证了我们的理论结果，并使用最先进的生成模型实现了公平的图像重建。

AI.tldr: 本文研究图像生成中的公平性问题，针对敏感属性（如种族、性别）的不确定性，提出新的公平性定义与实现方法。
AI.motivation: 传统公平性定义依赖预设的受保护群体，但这些分组具有历史和政治动机，且缺乏客观标准。例如，南亚与东亚人是否应归为同一群体存在争议，导致公平性难以统一界定。
AI.method: 提出条件比例代表性（Conditional Proportional Representation）定义，并通过后验抽样（Posterior Sampling）实现算法对敏感属性分组的无感知性。
AI.result: 实验验证了理论结论，利用先进生成模型实现了符合新公平性定义的图像重建，证明了传统人口统计公平性无法无感知实现，而新方法可行。
AI.conclusion: 本文揭示了传统公平性定义的局限性，提出可无感知处理敏感属性分组的新框架，为生成模型的公平性研究提供了理论与实践路径。

search_text: Fairness for Image Generation with Uncertain Sensitive Attributes 具有不确定敏感属性的图像生成的公平性 This work tackles the issue of fairness in the context of generative procedures, such as image super-resolution, which entail different definitions from the standard classification setting. Moreover, while traditional group fairness definitions are typically defined with respect to specified protected groups -- camouflaging the fact that these groupings are artificial and carry historical and political motivations -- we emphasize that there are no ground truth identities. For instance, should South and East Asians be viewed as a single group or separate groups? Should we consider one race as a whole or further split by gender? Choosing which groups are valid and who belongs in them is an impossible dilemma and being "fair" with respect to Asians may require being "unfair" with respect to South Asians. This motivates the introduction of definitions that allow algorithms to be \emph{oblivious} to the relevant groupings. We define several intuitive notions of group fairness and study their incompatibilities and trade-offs. We show that the natural extension of demographic parity is strongly dependent on the grouping, and \emph{impossible} to achieve obliviously. On the other hand, the conceptually new definition we introduce, Conditional Proportional Representation, can be achieved obliviously through Posterior Sampling. Our experiments validate our theoretical results and achieve fair image reconstruction using state-of-the-art generative models. 这项工作解决了生成过程中的公平性问题，例如图像超分辨率，这需要与标准分类设置不同的定义。此外，虽然传统的群体公平定义通常是针对特定的受保护群体来定义的——掩盖了这些群体是人为的并带有历史和政治动机的事实——但我们强调，不存在真正的身份。例如，南亚人和东亚人应该被视为一个群体还是单独的群体？我们应该将一个种族视为一个整体，还是进一步按性别划分？选择哪些群体是有效的以及谁属于其中是一个不可能的困境，对亚洲人“公平”可能需要对南亚人“不公平”。这促使引入定义，允许算法\emph{忘记}相关的分组。我们定义了群体公平的几个直观概念，并研究了它们的不兼容性和权衡。我们表明，人口均等的自然延伸强烈依赖于分组，并且\emph{不可能}在不知不觉中实现。另一方面，我们引入的概念上的新定义“条件比例表示”可以通过后验采样来实现。我们的实验验证了我们的理论结果，并使用最先进的生成模型实现了公平的图像重建。 本文研究图像生成中的公平性问题，针对敏感属性（如种族、性别）的不确定性，提出新的公平性定义与实现方法。 Ajil Jalal,Sushrut Karmalkar,Jessica Hoffmann,Alexandros G. authors_text: Aniket Shirke,Rebecca Golden,Mrinal Gautam,Angela Green-Miller,Matthew Caesar,Ryan N. Dilger
title: Vision-based Behavioral Recognition of Novelty Preference in Pigs
title_zh: 基于视觉的猪新奇偏好行为识别
comment: 5 pages, 7 figures, Accepted at the CVPR 2021 CV4Animals workshop
comments: 5 pages, 7 figures, Accepted at the CVPR 2021 CV4Animals workshop
abstract: Behavioral scoring of research data is crucial for extracting domain-specific metrics but is bottlenecked on the ability to analyze enormous volumes of information using human labor. Deep learning is widely viewed as a key advancement to relieve this bottleneck. We identify one such domain, where deep learning can be leveraged to alleviate the process of manual scoring. Novelty preference paradigms have been widely used to study recognition memory in pigs, but analysis of these videos requires human intervention. We introduce a subset of such videos in the form of the 'Pig Novelty Preference Behavior' (PNPB) dataset that is fully annotated with pig actions and keypoints. In order to demonstrate the application of state-of-the-art action recognition models on this dataset, we compare LRCN, C3D, and TSM on the basis of various analytical metrics and discuss common pitfalls of the models. Our methods achieve an accuracy of 93% and a mean Average Precision of 96% in estimating piglet behavior. We open-source our code and annotated dataset at https://github.com/AIFARMS/NOR-behavior-recognition
summary: Behavioral scoring of research data is crucial for extracting domain-specific metrics but is bottlenecked on the ability to analyze enormous volumes of information using human labor. Deep learning is widely viewed as a key advancement to relieve this bottleneck. We identify one such domain, where deep learning can be leveraged to alleviate the process of manual scoring. Novelty preference paradigms have been widely used to study recognition memory in pigs, but analysis of these videos requires human intervention. We introduce a subset of such videos in the form of the 'Pig Novelty Preference Behavior' (PNPB) dataset that is fully annotated with pig actions and keypoints. In order to demonstrate the application of state-of-the-art action recognition models on this dataset, we compare LRCN, C3D, and TSM on the basis of various analytical metrics and discuss common pitfalls of the models. Our methods achieve an accuracy of 93% and a mean Average Precision of 96% in estimating piglet behavior. We open-source our code and annotated dataset at https://github.com/AIFARMS/NOR-behavior-recognition
abstract_zh: 研究数据的行为评分对于提取特定领域的指标至关重要，但在使用人力分析大量信息的能力方面受到瓶颈。深度学习被广泛认为是缓解这一瓶颈的关键进步。我们确定了这样一个领域，可以利用深度学习来减轻手动评分的过程。新奇偏好范式已广泛用于研究猪的识别记忆，但对这些视频的分析需要人工干预。我们以“猪新奇偏好行为”（PNPB）数据集的形式引入此类视频的子集，该数据集完全用猪的动作和关键点进行注释。为了展示最先进的动作识别模型在此数据集上的应用，我们基于各种分析指标比较 LRCN、C3D 和 TSM，并讨论模型的常见缺陷。我们的方法在估计仔猪行为方面的准确度为 93%，平均精度为 96%。我们在 https://github.com/AIFARMS/NOR-behavior-recognition 开源我们的代码和带注释的数据集

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于视觉的深度学习方法，用于自动识别猪的新奇偏好行为，构建了标注数据集并验证了多种模型效果。
AI.motivation: 传统人工分析猪行为数据效率低且成本高，而深度学习可自动化处理视频数据，但缺乏针对猪新奇偏好行为的专用数据集和模型评估。
AI.method: 构建包含猪行为标注和关键点的PNPB数据集，采用LRCN、C3D和TSM三种动作识别模型进行对比实验，分析模型性能及常见问题。
AI.result: 在行为估计任务中达到93%准确率和96%平均精度，开源代码及数据集供研究使用。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习有效提升了猪新奇偏好行为分析的自动化水平，提供的数据集和模型对比为动物行为研究提供了新工具。

authors_text: Boyuan Zheng,Sunny Verma,Jianlong Zhou,Ivor Tsang,Fang Chen
title: Imitation Learning: Progress, Taxonomies and Challenges
title_zh: 模仿学习：进展、分类和挑战
abstract: Imitation learning aims to extract knowledge from human experts' demonstrations or artificially created agents in order to replicate their behaviors. Its success has been demonstrated in areas such as video games, autonomous driving, robotic simulations and object manipulation. However, this replicating process could be problematic, such as the performance is highly dependent on the demonstration quality, and most trained agents are limited to perform well in task-specific environments. In this survey, we provide a systematic review on imitation learning. We first introduce the background knowledge from development history and preliminaries, followed by presenting different taxonomies within Imitation Learning and key milestones of the field. We then detail challenges in learning strategies and present research opportunities with learning policy from suboptimal demonstration, voice instructions and other associated optimization schemes.
summary: Imitation learning aims to extract knowledge from human experts' demonstrations or artificially created agents in order to replicate their behaviors. Its success has been demonstrated in areas such as video games, autonomous driving, robotic simulations and object manipulation. However, this replicating process could be problematic, such as the performance is highly dependent on the demonstration quality, and most trained agents are limited to perform well in task-specific environments. In this survey, we provide a systematic review on imitation learning. We first introduce the background knowledge from development history and preliminaries, followed by presenting different taxonomies within Imitation Learning and key milestones of the field. We then detail challenges in learning strategies and present research opportunities with learning policy from suboptimal demonstration, voice instructions and other associated optimization schemes.
abstract_zh: 模仿学习旨在从人类专家的演示或人工创建的代理中提取知识，以复制他们的行为。它的成功已在视频游戏、自动驾驶、机器人模拟和对象操纵等领域得到证明。然而，这种复制过程可能存在问题，例如性能高度依赖于演示质量，并且大多数训练有素的智能体仅限于在特定任务环境中表现良好。在这项调查中，我们对模仿学习进行了系统回顾。我们首先介绍发展历史和预备知识的背景知识，然后介绍模仿学习中的不同分类法和该领域的关键里程碑。然后，我们详细介绍了学习策略中的挑战，并提出了来自次优演示、语音指令和其他相关优化方案的学习策略的研究机会。

AI.tldr: 本文系统综述了模仿学习的发展历程、分类体系及当前面临的主要挑战。
AI.motivation: 模仿学习虽在多个领域取得成功，但存在对示范质量高度依赖和环境适应性差等局限，需系统梳理其进展与挑战以推动后续研究。
AI.method: 通过梳理发展历史与基础理论，构建模仿学习的分类体系，分析关键里程碑，并探讨策略学习中的挑战与优化方案。
AI.result: 提出了模仿学习的系统分类框架，总结了领域内的重要进展，并指出了从次优示范、语音指令等角度的未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 模仿学习是实现复杂任务的重要途径，但需解决示范质量依赖性和环境泛化能力等问题，未来研究应关注多模态指令学习和策略优化方法。

search_text: Imitation Learning: Progress, Taxonomies and Challenges 模仿学习：进展、分类和挑战 Imitation learning aims to extract knowledge from human experts' demonstrations or artificially created agents in order to replicate their behaviors. Its success has been demonstrated in areas such as video games, autonomous driving, robotic simulations and object manipulation. However, this replicating process could be problematic, such as the performance is highly dependent on the demonstration quality, and most trained agents are limited to perform well in task-specific environments. In this survey, we provide a systematic review on imitation learning. We first introduce the background knowledge from development history and preliminaries, followed by presenting different taxonomies within Imitation Learning and key milestones of the field. We then detail challenges in learning strategies and present research opportunities with learning policy from suboptimal demonstration, voice instructions and other associated optimization schemes. 模仿学习旨在从人类专家的演示或人工创建的代理中提取知识，以复制他们的行为。它的成功已在视频游戏、自动驾驶、机器人模拟和对象操纵等领域得到证明。然而，这种复制过程可能存在问题，例如性能高度依赖于演示质量，并且大多数训练有素的智能体仅限于在特定任务环境中表现良好。在这项调查中，我们对模仿学习进行了系统回顾。我们首先介绍发展历史和预备知识的背景知识，然后介绍模仿学习中的不同分类法和该领域的关键里程碑。然后，我们详细介绍了学习策略中的挑战，并提出了来自次优演示、语音指令和其他相关优化方案的学习策略的研究机会。 本文系统综述了模仿学习的发展历程、分类体系及当前面临的主要挑战。 Boyuan Zheng,Sunny Verma,Jianlong Zhou,Ivor Tsang,Fang Chen cs.LG,cs.RO

title: Deformed2Self: Self-Supervised Denoising for Dynamic Medical Imaging
title_zh: Deformed2Self：动态医学成像的自监督去噪
abstract: Image denoising is of great importance for medical imaging system, since it can improve image quality for disease diagnosis and downstream image analyses. In a variety of applications, dynamic imaging techniques are utilized to capture the time-varying features of the subject, where multiple images are acquired for the same subject at different time points. Although signal-to-noise ratio of each time frame is usually limited by the short acquisition time, the correlation among different time frames can be exploited to improve denoising results with shared information across time frames. With the success of neural networks in computer vision, supervised deep learning methods show prominent performance in single-image denoising, which rely on large datasets with clean-vs-noisy image pairs. Recently, several self-supervised deep denoising models have been proposed, achieving promising results without needing the pairwise ground truth of clean images. In the field of multi-image denoising, however, very few works have been done on extracting correlated information from multiple slices for denoising using self-supervised deep learning methods. In this work, we propose Deformed2Self, an end-to-end self-supervised deep learning framework for dynamic imaging denoising. It combines single-image and multi-image denoising to improve image quality and use a spatial transformer network to model motion between different slices. Further, it only requires a single noisy image with a few auxiliary observations at different time frames for training and inference. Evaluations on phantom and in vivo data with different noise statistics show that our method has comparable performance to other state-of-the-art unsupervised or self-supervised denoising methods and outperforms under high noise levels.
summary: Image denoising is of great importance for medical imaging system, since it can improve image quality for disease diagnosis and downstream image analyses. In a variety of applications, dynamic imaging techniques are utilized to capture the time-varying features of the subject, where multiple images are acquired for the same subject at different time points. Although signal-to-noise ratio of each time frame is usually limited by the short acquisition time, the correlation among different time frames can be exploited to improve denoising results with shared information across time frames. With the success of neural networks in computer vision, supervised deep learning methods show prominent performance in single-image denoising, which rely on large datasets with clean-vs-noisy image pairs. Recently, several self-supervised deep denoising models have been proposed, achieving promising results without needing the pairwise ground truth of clean images. In the field of multi-image denoising, however, very few works have been done on extracting correlated information from multiple slices for denoising using self-supervised deep learning methods. In this work, we propose Deformed2Self, an end-to-end self-supervised deep learning framework for dynamic imaging denoising. It combines single-image and multi-image denoising to improve image quality and use a spatial transformer network to model motion between different slices. Further, it only requires a single noisy image with a few auxiliary observations at different time frames for training and inference. Evaluations on phantom and in vivo data with different noise statistics show that our method has comparable performance to other state-of-the-art unsupervised or self-supervised denoising methods and outperforms under high noise levels.
abstract_zh: 图像去噪对于医学成像系统非常重要，因为它可以提高疾病诊断和下游图像分析的图像质量。在各种应用中，动态成像技术用于捕获对象随时间变化的特征，其中在不同时间点获取同一对象的多个图像。尽管每个时间帧的信噪比通常受到较短采集时间的限制，但可以利用不同时间帧之间的相关性，通过跨时间帧共享信息来改善去噪结果。随着神经网络在计算机视觉领域的成功，有监督的深度学习方法在单图像去噪方面表现出了突出的性能，这些方法依赖于具有干净与噪声图像对的大型数据集。最近，已经提出了几种自监督深度去噪模型，在不需要干净图像的成对地面实况的情况下取得了有希望的结果。然而，在多图像去噪领域，利用自监督深度学习方法从多个切片中提取相关信息进行去噪的工作还很少。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Deformed2Self，一种用于动态图像去噪的端到端自监督深度学习框架。它结合了单图像和多图像去噪以提高图像质量，并使用空间变换网络对不同切片之间的运动进行建模。此外，它只需要单个噪声图像以及不同时间帧的一些辅助观察来进行训练和推理。对具有不同噪声统计数据的体模和体内数据的评估表明，我们的方法具有与其他最先进的无监督或自监督去噪方法相当的性能，并且在高噪声水平下表现更好。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种自监督去噪框架Deformed2Self，用于动态医学影像中的噪声去除。
AI.motivation: 动态医学影像需处理时间序列图像间的相关性，但现有自监督方法未能有效利用多帧信息，且依赖成对的干净-噪声图像数据。
AI.method: 结合单帧与多帧去噪，通过空间变换网络建模不同时间点图像间的运动关系，仅需单帧噪声图像及少量辅助观测数据进行训练与推理。
AI.result: 在不同噪声水平的仿真与真实医学数据上，该方法性能与先进无监督/自监督方法相当，并在高噪声场景下表现更优。
AI.conclusion: Deformed2Self通过自监督学习有效利用动态影像的时序相关性，在无需成对标注数据的情况下实现了高质量去噪，适用于临床动态医学影像处理。

authors_text: Balamurali B T,Hwan Ing Hee,Saumitra Kapoor,Oon Hoe Teoh,Sung Shin Teng,Khai Pin Lee,Dorien Herremans,Jer Ming Chen
title: Deep Neural Network Based Respiratory Pathology Classification Using Cough Sounds
title_zh: 使用咳嗽声音进行基于深度神经网络的呼吸病理学分类
abstract: Intelligent systems are transforming the world, as well as our healthcare system. We propose a deep learning-based cough sound classification model that can distinguish between children with healthy versus pathological coughs such as asthma, upper respiratory tract infection (URTI), and lower respiratory tract infection (LRTI). In order to train a deep neural network model, we collected a new dataset of cough sounds, labelled with clinician's diagnosis. The chosen model is a bidirectional long-short term memory network (BiLSTM) based on Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) features. The resulting trained model when trained for classifying two classes of coughs -- healthy or pathology (in general or belonging to a specific respiratory pathology), reaches accuracy exceeding 84\% when classifying cough to the label provided by the physicians' diagnosis. In order to classify subject's respiratory pathology condition, results of multiple cough epochs per subject were combined. The resulting prediction accuracy exceeds 91\% for all three respiratory pathologies. However, when the model is trained to classify and discriminate among the four classes of coughs, overall accuracy dropped: one class of pathological coughs are often misclassified as other. However, if one consider the healthy cough classified as healthy and pathological cough classified to have some kind of pathologies, then the overall accuracy of four class model is above 84\%. A longitudinal study of MFCC feature space when comparing pathological and recovered coughs collected from the same subjects revealed the fact that pathological cough irrespective of the underlying conditions occupy the same feature space making it harder to differentiate only using MFCC features.
summary: Intelligent systems are transforming the world, as well as our healthcare system. We propose a deep learning-based cough sound classification model that can distinguish between children with healthy versus pathological coughs such as asthma, upper respiratory tract infection (URTI), and lower respiratory tract infection (LRTI). In order to train a deep neural network model, we collected a new dataset of cough sounds, labelled with clinician's diagnosis. The chosen model is a bidirectional long-short term memory network (BiLSTM) based on Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) features. The resulting trained model when trained for classifying two classes of coughs -- healthy or pathology (in general or belonging to a specific respiratory pathology), reaches accuracy exceeding 84\% when classifying cough to the label provided by the physicians' diagnosis. In order to classify subject's respiratory pathology condition, results of multiple cough epochs per subject were combined. The resulting prediction accuracy exceeds 91\% for all three respiratory pathologies. However, when the model is trained to classify and discriminate among the four classes of coughs, overall accuracy dropped: one class of pathological coughs are often misclassified as other. However, if one consider the healthy cough classified as healthy and pathological cough classified to have some kind of pathologies, then the overall accuracy of four class model is above 84\%. A longitudinal study of MFCC feature space when comparing pathological and recovered coughs collected from the same subjects revealed the fact that pathological cough irrespective of the underlying conditions occupy the same feature space making it harder to differentiate only using MFCC features.
abstract_zh: 智能系统正在改变世界以及我们的医疗保健系统。我们提出了一种基于深度学习的咳嗽声音分类模型，可以区分患有健康咳嗽和病理性咳嗽的儿童，例如哮喘、上呼吸道感染（URTI）和下呼吸道感染（LRTI）。为了训练深度神经网络模型，我们收集了一个新的咳嗽声音数据集，并标有临床医生的诊断。所选模型是基于梅尔频率倒谱系数 (MFCC) 特征的双向长短期记忆网络 (BiLSTM)。所得到的训练模型在训练用于对两类咳嗽（健康或病理学（一般或属于特定的呼吸道病理学））进行分类时，在根据医生诊断提供的标签对咳嗽进行分类时达到超过 84% 的准确度。为了对受试者的呼吸道病理状况进行分类，合并了每个受试者的多个咳嗽时期的结果。对于所有三种呼吸系统疾病，最终的预测准确度均超过 91%。然而，当训练模型对四类咳嗽进行分类和区分时，总体准确性下降：一类病理性咳嗽经常被错误地分类为另一类。然而，如果考虑将健康咳嗽分类为健康，将病理性咳嗽分类为某种病理，则四类模型的总体准确率在 84% 以上。对 MFCC 特征空间的纵向研究在比较从同一受试者收集的病理性咳嗽和康复咳嗽时发现，无论基础条件如何，病理性咳嗽都占据相同的特征空间，因此仅使用 MFCC 特征很难区分。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于深度神经网络的咳嗽声分类模型，用于区分儿童健康咳嗽与哮喘、上呼吸道感染、下呼吸道感染等病理咳嗽。
AI.motivation: 通过智能系统实现医疗自动化，利用咳嗽声进行呼吸系统疾病早期筛查，减少对传统医疗资源的依赖。
AI.method: 采集临床诊断标注的咳嗽声数据集，采用双向长短期记忆网络（BiLSTM）结合梅尔频率倒谱系数（MFCC）特征进行训练，通过多轮咳嗽数据融合提升分类精度。
AI.result: 二分类模型准确率超84%，三类病理分类准确率超91%；四分类模型整体准确率下降至84%以上，因不同病理咳嗽在MFCC特征空间中分布重叠。
AI.conclusion: MFCC特征难以区分不同病理咳嗽，但模型可有效用于健康/病理二分类，为临床辅助诊断提供可行方案。

tldr: 本文提出基于深度神经网络的咳嗽声分类模型，用于区分儿童健康咳嗽与哮喘、上呼吸道感染、下呼吸道感染等病理咳嗽。
motivation: 通过智能系统实现医疗自动化，利用咳嗽声进行呼吸系统疾病早期筛查，减少对传统医疗资源的依赖。
method: 采集临床诊断标注的咳嗽声数据集，采用双向长短期记忆网络（BiLSTM）结合梅尔频率倒谱系数（MFCC）特征进行训练，通过多轮咳嗽数据融合提升分类精度。
result: 二分类模型准确率超84%，三类病理分类准确率超91%；四分类模型整体准确率下降至84%以上，因不同病理咳嗽在MFCC特征空间中分布重叠。
conclusion: MFCC特征难以区分不同病理咳嗽，但模型可有效用于健康/病理二分类，为临床辅助诊断提供可行方案。
search_text: Deep Neural Network Based Respiratory Pathology Classification Using Cough Sounds 使用咳嗽声音进行基于深度神经网络的呼吸病理学分类 Intelligent systems are transforming the world, as well as our healthcare system. We propose a deep learning-based cough sound classification model that can distinguish between children with healthy versus pathological coughs such as asthma, upper respiratory tract infection (URTI), and lower respiratory tract infection (LRTI). In order to train a deep neural network model, we collected a new dataset of cough sounds, labelled with clinician's diagnosis. The chosen model is a bidirectional long-short term memory network (BiLSTM) based on Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) features. The resulting trained model when trained for classifying two classes of coughs -- healthy or pathology (in general or belonging to a specific respiratory pathology), reaches accuracy exceeding 84\% when classifying cough to the label provided by the physicians' diagnosis. In order to classify subject's respiratory pathology condition, results of multiple cough epochs per subject were combined. The resulting prediction accuracy exceeds 91\% for all three respiratory pathologies. However, when the model is trained to classify and discriminate among the four classes of coughs, overall accuracy dropped: one class of pathological coughs are often misclassified as other. However, if one consider the healthy cough classified as healthy and pathological cough classified to have some kind of pathologies, then the overall accuracy of four class model is above 84\%. A longitudinal study of MFCC feature space when comparing pathological and recovered coughs collected from the same subjects revealed the fact that pathological cough irrespective of the underlying conditions occupy the same feature space making it harder to differentiate only using MFCC features. 智能系统正在改变世界以及我们的医疗保健系统。我们提出了一种基于深度学习的咳嗽声音分类模型，可以区分患有健康咳嗽和病理性咳嗽的儿童，例如哮喘、上呼吸道感染（URTI）和下呼吸道感染（LRTI）。为了训练深度神经网络模型，我们收集了一个新的咳嗽声音数据集，并标有临床医生的诊断。所选模型是基于梅尔频率倒谱系数 (MFCC) 特征的双向长短期记忆网络 (BiLSTM)。所得到的训练模型在训练用于对两类咳嗽（健康或病理学（一般或属于特定的呼吸道病理学））进行分类时，在根据医生诊断提供的标签对咳嗽进行分类时达到超过 84% 的准确度。为了对受试者的呼吸道病理状况进行分类，合并了每个受试者的多个咳嗽时期的结果。对于所有三种呼吸系统疾病，最终的预测准确度均超过 91%。然而，当训练模型对四类咳嗽进行分类和区分时，总体准确性下降：一类病理性咳嗽经常被错误地分类为另一类。然而，如果考虑将健康咳嗽分类为健康，将病理性咳嗽分类为某种病理，则四类模型的总体准确率在 84% 以上。对 MFCC 特征空间的纵向研究在比较从同一受试者收集的病理性咳嗽和康复咳嗽时发现，无论基础条件如何，病理性咳嗽都占据相同的特征空间，因此仅使用 MFCC 特征很难区分。 本文提出基于深度神经网络的咳嗽声分类模型，用于区分儿童健康咳嗽与哮喘、上呼吸道感染、下呼吸道感染等病理咳嗽。 Balamurali B T,Hwan Ing Hee,Saumitra Kapoor,Oon Hoe Teoh,Sung Shin Teng,Khai Pin Lee,Dorien Herremans,Jer Ming Chen cs.LG,cs.MM,cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Boyuan Feng,Yuke Wang,Tong Geng,Ang Li,Yufei Ding
title: APNN-TC: Accelerating Arbitrary Precision Neural Networks on Ampere GPU Tensor Cores
title_zh: APNN-TC：在 Ampere GPU 张量核心上加速任意精度神经网络
abstract: Over the years, accelerating neural networks with quantization has been widely studied. Unfortunately, prior efforts with diverse precisions (e.g., 1-bit weights and 2-bit activations) are usually restricted by limited precision support on GPUs (e.g., int1 and int4). To break such restrictions, we introduce the first Arbitrary Precision Neural Network framework (APNN-TC) to fully exploit quantization benefits on Ampere GPU Tensor Cores. Specifically, APNN-TC first incorporates a novel emulation algorithm to support arbitrary short bit-width computation with int1 compute primitives and XOR/AND Boolean operations. Second, APNN-TC integrates arbitrary precision layer designs to efficiently map our emulation algorithm to Tensor Cores with novel batching strategies and specialized memory organization. Third, APNN-TC embodies a novel arbitrary precision NN design to minimize memory access across layers and further improve performance. Extensive evaluations show that APNN-TC can achieve significant speedup over CUTLASS kernels and various NN models, such as ResNet and VGG.
summary: Over the years, accelerating neural networks with quantization has been widely studied. Unfortunately, prior efforts with diverse precisions (e.g., 1-bit weights and 2-bit activations) are usually restricted by limited precision support on GPUs (e.g., int1 and int4). To break such restrictions, we introduce the first Arbitrary Precision Neural Network framework (APNN-TC) to fully exploit quantization benefits on Ampere GPU Tensor Cores. Specifically, APNN-TC first incorporates a novel emulation algorithm to support arbitrary short bit-width computation with int1 compute primitives and XOR/AND Boolean operations. Second, APNN-TC integrates arbitrary precision layer designs to efficiently map our emulation algorithm to Tensor Cores with novel batching strategies and specialized memory organization. Third, APNN-TC embodies a novel arbitrary precision NN design to minimize memory access across layers and further improve performance. Extensive evaluations show that APNN-TC can achieve significant speedup over CUTLASS kernels and various NN models, such as ResNet and VGG.
abstract_zh: 多年来，通过量化加速神经网络已得到广泛研究。不幸的是，先前具有不同精度（例如，1 位权重和 2 位激活）的努力通常受到 GPU 上有限精度支持（例如，int1 和 int4）的限制。为了打破这些限制，我们引入了第一个任意精度神经网络框架 (APNN-TC)，以充分利用 Ampere GPU Tensor Core 上的量化优势。具体来说，APNN-TC 首先采用了一种新颖的仿真算法，以支持使用 int1 计算原语和 XOR/AND 布尔运算的任意短位宽计算。其次，APNN-TC 集成了任意精度的层设计，通过新颖的批处理策略和专门的内存组织，有效地将我们的仿真算法映射到 Tensor Core。第三，APNN-TC 体现了一种新颖的任意精度神经网络设计，可最大限度地减少跨层内存访问并进一步提高性能。广泛的评估表明，APNN-TC 可以比 CUTLASS 内核和各种神经网络模型（例如 ResNet 和 VGG）实现显着的加速。

AI.tldr: 本文提出APNN-TC框架，通过任意精度计算加速Ampere GPU张量核心上的神经网络。
AI.motivation: 现有GPU对低精度计算支持有限（如int1/int4），难以充分发挥量化神经网络的性能潜力。
AI.method: 设计新型模拟算法结合布尔运算实现任意位宽计算，优化分层结构与内存组织策略，减少跨层内存访问。
AI.result: 在ResNet/VGG等模型上显著优于CUTLASS内核，实现加速效果。
AI.conclusion: APNN-TC有效突破GPU精度限制，为任意精度神经网络加速提供了新方法。

authors_text: Yuehai Chen,Jing Yang,Dong Zhang,Kun Zhang,Badong Chen,Shaoyi Du
title: Region-Aware Network: Model Human's Top-Down Visual Perception Mechanism for Crowd Counting
title_zh: 区域感知网络：模拟人类自上而下的视觉感知机制以进行人群计数
abstract: Background noise and scale variation are common problems that have been long recognized in crowd counting. Humans glance at a crowd image and instantly know the approximate number of human and where they are through attention the crowd regions and the congestion degree of crowd regions with a global receptive field. Hence, in this paper, we propose a novel feedback network with Region-Aware block called RANet by modeling humans Top-Down visual perception mechanism. Firstly, we introduce a feedback architecture to generate priority maps that provide prior about candidate crowd regions in input images. The prior enables the RANet pay more attention to crowd regions. Then we design Region-Aware block that could adaptively encode the contextual information into input images through global receptive field. More specifically, we scan the whole input images and its priority maps in the form of column vector to obtain a relevance matrix estimating their similarity. The relevance matrix obtained would be utilized to build global relationships between pixels. Our method outperforms state-of-the-art crowd counting methods on several public datasets.
summary: Background noise and scale variation are common problems that have been long recognized in crowd counting. Humans glance at a crowd image and instantly know the approximate number of human and where they are through attention the crowd regions and the congestion degree of crowd regions with a global receptive field. Hence, in this paper, we propose a novel feedback network with Region-Aware block called RANet by modeling humans Top-Down visual perception mechanism. Firstly, we introduce a feedback architecture to generate priority maps that provide prior about candidate crowd regions in input images. The prior enables the RANet pay more attention to crowd regions. Then we design Region-Aware block that could adaptively encode the contextual information into input images through global receptive field. More specifically, we scan the whole input images and its priority maps in the form of column vector to obtain a relevance matrix estimating their similarity. The relevance matrix obtained would be utilized to build global relationships between pixels. Our method outperforms state-of-the-art crowd counting methods on several public datasets.
abstract_zh: 背景噪声和尺度变化是人群计数中早已认识到的常见问题。人类看一眼人群图像，通过全局感受野关注人群区域和人群区域的拥挤程度，立即知道大概有多少人以及他们在哪里。因此，在本文中，我们通过模拟人类自上而下的视觉感知机制，提出了一种具有区域感知块的新型反馈网络，称为 RANet。首先，我们引入反馈架构来生成优先级图，该优先级图提供输入图像中候选人群区域的先验信息。先验使得 RANet 更加关注人群区域。然后我们设计了区域感知块，它可以通过全局感受野自适应地将上下文信息编码到输入图像中。更具体地说，我们以列向量的形式扫描整个输入图像及其优先级图，以获得估计它们相似度的相关矩阵。获得的相关性矩阵将用于建立像素之间的全局关系。我们的方法在多个公共数据集上优于最先进的人群计数方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为RANet的新型反馈网络，通过模拟人类自上而下的视觉感知机制，解决人群计数中的背景噪声和尺度变化问题。
AI.motivation: 传统人群计数方法难以有效处理背景噪声和尺度变化，而人类通过关注人群区域及拥挤程度可快速估算数量，因此需建模类似机制提升算法性能。
AI.method: 设计包含反馈架构的RANet，生成优先图以定位人群区域，并通过Region-Aware模块利用全局感受野编码上下文信息，基于相关矩阵建立像素间全局关系。
AI.result: 在多个公开数据集上，RANet的性能优于现有先进人群计数方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过模拟人类视觉机制，RANet有效提升了人群计数的准确性，未来可进一步优化模块设计或扩展至其他视觉任务。

title: CxSE: Chest X-ray Slow Encoding CNN forCOVID-19 Diagnosis
title_zh: CxSE：用于 COVID-19 诊断的胸部 X 射线慢编码 CNN
abstract: The coronavirus continues to disrupt our everyday lives as it spreads at an exponential rate. It needs to be detected quickly in order to quarantine positive patients so as to avoid further spread. This work proposes a new convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture called 'slow Encoding CNN. The proposed model's best performance wrt Sensitivity, Positive Predictive Value (PPV) found to be SP=0.67, PP=0.98, SN=0.96, and PN=0.52 on AI AGAINST COVID19 - Screening X-ray images for COVID-19 Infections competition's test data samples. SP and PP stand for the Sensitivity and PPV of the COVID-19 positive class, while PN and SN stand for the Sensitivity and PPV of the COVID-19 negative class.

SP and PP stand for the Sensitivity and PPV of the COVID-19 positive class, while PN and SN stand for the Sensitivity and PPV of the COVID-19 negative class.
abstract_zh: 冠状病毒以指数速度传播，继续扰乱我们的日常生活。需要快速检测，以便隔离阳性患者，避免进一步传播。这项工作提出了一种新的卷积神经网络 (CNN) 架构，称为“慢编码 CNN”。在 AI AGAINST COVID19 - 针对 COVID-19 感染筛查 X 射线图像竞赛的测试数据样本上，所提出的模型在灵敏度、阳性预测值 (PPV) 方面的最佳性能为 SP=0.67、PP=0.98、SN=0.96 和 PN=0.52。 SP 和 PP 代表 COVID-19 阳性类别的敏感度和 PPV，而 PN 和 SN 代表 COVID-19 阴性类别的敏感度和 PPV。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于胸部X光图像的慢编码卷积神经网络（CxSE），用于快速诊断COVID-19。
AI.motivation: 新冠病毒传播迅速，需快速检测以隔离感染者，传统方法存在效率或准确性不足的问题。
AI.method: 设计新型CNN架构'慢编码CNN'，通过AI AGAINST COVID19竞赛的X光数据集进行训练与测试。
AI.result: 在测试数据中，模型对COVID-19阳性样本的灵敏度(SN)达0.96，阳性预测值(PPV)为0.98；对阴性样本的灵敏度为0.52，PPV为0.67。
AI.conclusion: 该模型在竞赛数据集上展现了较高的诊断准确性，尤其在识别阳性病例方面表现突出，为临床快速筛查提供了可行方案。

title: Neural Fashion Image Captioning : Accounting for Data Diversity
title_zh: 神经时尚图像字幕：考虑数据多样性
abstract: Image captioning has increasingly large domains of application, and fashion is not an exception. Having automatic item descriptions is of great interest for fashion web platforms, sometimes hosting hundreds of thousands of images. This paper is one of the first to tackle image captioning for fashion images. To address dataset diversity issues, we introduced the InFashAIv1 dataset containing almost 16.000 African fashion item images with their titles, prices, and general descriptions. We also used the well-known DeepFashion dataset in addition to InFashAIv1. Captions are generated using the Show and Tell model made of CNN encoder and RNN Decoder. We showed that jointly training the model on both datasets improves captions quality for African style fashion images, suggesting a transfer learning from Western style data. The InFashAIv1 dataset is released on Github to encourage works with more diversity inclusion.
summary: Image captioning has increasingly large domains of application, and fashion is not an exception. Having automatic item descriptions is of great interest for fashion web platforms, sometimes hosting hundreds of thousands of images. This paper is one of the first to tackle image captioning for fashion images. To address dataset diversity issues, we introduced the InFashAIv1 dataset containing almost 16.000 African fashion item images with their titles, prices, and general descriptions. We also used the well-known DeepFashion dataset in addition to InFashAIv1. Captions are generated using the Show and Tell model made of CNN encoder and RNN Decoder. We showed that jointly training the model on both datasets improves captions quality for African style fashion images, suggesting a transfer learning from Western style data. The InFashAIv1 dataset is released on Github to encourage works with more diversity inclusion.
abstract_zh: 图像字幕的应用领域越来越广泛，时尚也不例外。时尚网络平台对自动商品描述非常感兴趣，有时会托管数十万张图像。这篇论文是最早解决时尚图像图像字幕问题的论文之一。为了解决数据集多样性问题，我们引入了 InFashAIv1 数据集，其中包含近 16,000 个非洲时尚商品图像及其标题、价格和一般描述。除了InFashAIv1之外，我们还使用了著名的DeepFashion数据集。字幕是使用由 CNN 编码器和 RNN 解码器组成的 Show and Tell 模型生成的。我们表明，在两个数据集上联合训练模型可以提高非洲风格时尚图像的字幕质量，这表明从西方风格数据进行迁移学习。 InFashAIv1 数据集在 Github 上发布，以鼓励更具多样性包容性的作品。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过神经网络模型解决时尚图像字幕生成中的数据多样性问题，特别关注非洲风格服饰图像的自动描述。
AI.motivation: 现有时尚图像字幕数据集以西方风格为主，缺乏对非洲等多样化时尚元素的覆盖，导致模型在跨文化场景下表现不足。
AI.method: 构建包含16000张非洲时尚图像的InFashAIv1数据集，结合DeepFashion数据集，采用CNN-RNN的Show and Tell模型进行跨数据集联合训练。
AI.result: 联合训练显著提升非洲风格图像字幕生成质量，验证了从西方风格数据到非洲风格的迁移学习有效性。
AI.conclusion: 数据多样性对模型性能有重要影响，公开的InFashAIv1数据集为跨文化时尚图像研究提供了新基础。

authors_text: Zejian Chen,Wei Zhuo,Tianfu Wang,Wufeng Xue,Dong Ni
title: Bootstrap Representation Learning for Segmentation on Medical Volumes and Sequences
title_zh: 用于医学体积和序列分割的引导表示学习
abstract: In this work, we propose a novel straightforward method for medical volume and sequence segmentation with limited annotations. To avert laborious annotating, the recent success of self-supervised learning(SSL) motivates the pre-training on unlabeled data. Despite its success, it is still challenging to adapt typical SSL methods to volume/sequence segmentation, due to their lack of mining on local semantic discrimination and rare exploitation on volume and sequence structures. Based on the continuity between slices/frames and the common spatial layout of organs across volumes/sequences, we introduced a novel bootstrap self-supervised representation learning method by leveraging the predictable possibility of neighboring slices. At the core of our method is a simple and straightforward dense self-supervision on the predictions of local representations and a strategy of predicting locals based on global context, which enables stable and reliable supervision for both global and local representation mining among volumes. Specifically, we first proposed an asymmetric network with an attention-guided predictor to enforce distance-specific prediction and supervision on slices within and across volumes/sequences. Secondly, we introduced a novel prototype-based foreground-background calibration module to enhance representation consistency. The two parts are trained jointly on labeled and unlabeled data. When evaluated on three benchmark datasets of medical volumes and sequences, our model outperforms existing methods with a large margin of 4.5\% DSC on ACDC, 1.7\% on Prostate, and 2.3\% on CAMUS. Intensive evaluations reveals the effectiveness and superiority of our method.
summary: In this work, we propose a novel straightforward method for medical volume and sequence segmentation with limited annotations. To avert laborious annotating, the recent success of self-supervised learning(SSL) motivates the pre-training on unlabeled data. Despite its success, it is still challenging to adapt typical SSL methods to volume/sequence segmentation, due to their lack of mining on local semantic discrimination and rare exploitation on volume and sequence structures. Based on the continuity between slices/frames and the common spatial layout of organs across volumes/sequences, we introduced a novel bootstrap self-supervised representation learning method by leveraging the predictable possibility of neighboring slices. At the core of our method is a simple and straightforward dense self-supervision on the predictions of local representations and a strategy of predicting locals based on global context, which enables stable and reliable supervision for both global and local representation mining among volumes. Specifically, we first proposed an asymmetric network with an attention-guided predictor to enforce distance-specific prediction and supervision on slices within and across volumes/sequences. Secondly, we introduced a novel prototype-based foreground-background calibration module to enhance representation consistency. The two parts are trained jointly on labeled and unlabeled data. When evaluated on three benchmark datasets of medical volumes and sequences, our model outperforms existing methods with a large margin of 4.5\% DSC on ACDC, 1.7\% on Prostate, and 2.3\% on CAMUS. Intensive evaluations reveals the effectiveness and superiority of our method.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的简单方法，用于具有有限注释的医学体积和序列分割。为了避免繁琐的注释，最近成功的自监督学习（SSL）激发了对未标记数据的预训练。尽管取得了成功，但将典型的 SSL 方法应用于体/序列分割仍然具有挑战性，因为它们缺乏对局部语义区分的挖掘，并且很少对体和序列结构进行利用。基于切片/帧之间的连续性以及跨体积/序列的器官的共同空间布局，我们通过利用相邻切片的可预测可能性，引入了一种新颖的引导自监督表示学习方法。我们方法的核心是对局部表示的预测进行简单直接的密集自监督，以及基于全局上下文预测局部的策略，这使得对体之间的全局和局部表示挖掘能够进行稳定可靠的监督。具体来说，我们首先提出了一个带有注意力引导预测器的非对称网络，以对卷/序列内和跨卷/序列的切片实施特定于距离的预测和监督。其次，我们引入了一种新颖的基于原型的前景-背景校准模块来增强表示一致性。这两部分在标记和未标记数据上联合训练。在三个医疗体积和序列基准数据集上进行评估时，我们的模型优于现有方法，在 ACDC 上 DSC 为 4.5\%，在前列腺上为 1.7\%，在 CAMUS 上为 2.3\%。深入的评估揭示了我们方法的有效性和优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种用于医学体积和序列分割的自监督表示学习方法，以解决标注数据有限的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统自监督学习方法在医学体积/序列分割中效果有限，因未充分挖掘局部语义差异和体积/序列结构特性。
AI.method: 设计了基于邻近切片可预测性的引导自监督学习框架，包含异步网络结构和基于原型的前景-背景校准模块，联合利用标注与未标注数据。
AI.result: 在ACDC、Prostate、CAMUS三个数据集上分别以4.5%、1.7%、2.3%的Dice系数超越现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过局部-全局协同监督机制有效提升了医学图像分割性能，验证了自监督学习在医学领域的有效性。

conclusion: 该方法通过局部-全局协同监督机制有效提升了医学图像分割性能，验证了自监督学习在医学领域的有效性。
search_text: Bootstrap Representation Learning for Segmentation on Medical Volumes and Sequences 用于医学体积和序列分割的引导表示学习 In this work, we propose a novel straightforward method for medical volume and sequence segmentation with limited annotations. To avert laborious annotating, the recent success of self-supervised learning(SSL) motivates the pre-training on unlabeled data. Despite its success, it is still challenging to adapt typical SSL methods to volume/sequence segmentation, due to their lack of mining on local semantic discrimination and rare exploitation on volume and sequence structures. Based on the continuity between slices/frames and the common spatial layout of organs across volumes/sequences, we introduced a novel bootstrap self-supervised representation learning method by leveraging the predictable possibility of neighboring slices. At the core of our method is a simple and straightforward dense self-supervision on the predictions of local representations and a strategy of predicting locals based on global context, which enables stable and reliable supervision for both global and local representation mining among volumes. Specifically, we first proposed an asymmetric network with an attention-guided predictor to enforce distance-specific prediction and supervision on slices within and across volumes/sequences. Secondly, we introduced a novel prototype-based foreground-background calibration module to enhance representation consistency. The two parts are trained jointly on labeled and unlabeled data. When evaluated on three benchmark datasets of medical volumes and sequences, our model outperforms existing methods with a large margin of 4.5\% DSC on ACDC, 1.7\% on Prostate, and 2.3\% on CAMUS. Intensive evaluations reveals the effectiveness and superiority of our method. 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的简单方法，用于具有有限注释的医学体积和序列分割。为了避免繁琐的注释，最近成功的自监督学习（SSL）激发了对未标记数据的预训练。尽管取得了成功，但将典型的 SSL 方法应用于体/序列分割仍然具有挑战性，因为它们缺乏对局部语义区分的挖掘，并且很少对体和序列结构进行利用。基于切片/帧之间的连续性以及跨体积/序列的器官的共同空间布局，我们通过利用相邻切片的可预测可能性，引入了一种新颖的引导自监督表示学习方法。我们方法的核心是对局部表示的预测进行简单直接的密集自监督，以及基于全局上下文预测局部的策略，这使得对体之间的全局和局部表示挖掘能够进行稳定可靠的监督。具体来说，我们首先提出了一个带有注意力引导预测器的非对称网络，以对卷/序列内和跨卷/序列的切片实施特定于距离的预测和监督。其次，我们引入了一种新颖的基于原型的前景-背景校准模块来增强表示一致性。这两部分在标记和未标记数据上联合训练。在三个医疗体积和序列基准数据集上进行评估时，我们的模型优于现有方法，在 ACDC 上 DSC 为 4.5\%，在前列腺上为 1.7\%，在 CAMUS 上为 2.3\%。深入的评估揭示了我们方法的有效性和优越性。 本文提出一种用于医学体积和序列分割的自监督表示学习方法，以解决标注数据有限的问题。 Zejian Chen,Wei Zhuo,Tianfu Wang,Wufeng Xue,Dong Ni cs.CV

title: Width-based Lookaheads with Learnt Base Policies and Heuristics Over the Atari-2600 Benchmark
title_zh: 基于 Atari-2600 基准的基于宽度的预测，具有学习的基本策略和启发式
abstract: We propose new width-based planning and learning algorithms inspired from a careful analysis of the design decisions made by previous width-based planners. The algorithms are applied over the Atari-2600 games and our best performing algorithm, Novelty guided Critical Path Learning (N-CPL), outperforms the previously introduced width-based planning and learning algorithms $π$-IW(1), $π$-IW(1)+ and $π$-HIW(n, 1). Furthermore, we present a taxonomy of the Atari-2600 games according to some of their defining characteristics. This analysis of the games provides further insight into the behaviour and performance of the algorithms introduced. Namely, for games with large branching factors, and games with sparse meaningful rewards, N-CPL outperforms $π$-IW, $π$-IW(1)+ and $π$-HIW(n, 1).
summary: We propose new width-based planning and learning algorithms inspired from a careful analysis of the design decisions made by previous width-based planners. The algorithms are applied over the Atari-2600 games and our best performing algorithm, Novelty guided Critical Path Learning (N-CPL), outperforms the previously introduced width-based planning and learning algorithms $π$-IW(1), $π$-IW(1)+ and $π$-HIW(n, 1). Furthermore, we present a taxonomy of the Atari-2600 games according to some of their defining characteristics. This analysis of the games provides further insight into the behaviour and performance of the algorithms introduced. Namely, for games with large branching factors, and games with sparse meaningful rewards, N-CPL outperforms $π$-IW, $π$-IW(1)+ and $π$-HIW(n, 1).
abstract_zh: 我们提出了新的基于宽度的规划和学习算法，其灵感来自于对先前基于宽度的规划器所做的设计决策的仔细分析。这些算法应用于 Atari-2600 游戏，我们性能最佳的算法，新颖性引导的关键路径学习 (N-CPL)，优于之前引入的基于宽度的规划和学习算法 $π$-IW(1)、$π$-IW(1)+ 和 $π$-HIW(n, 1)。此外，我们还根据 Atari-2600 游戏的一些定义特征对它们进行了分类。对游戏的分析可以进一步深入了解所引入算法的行为和性能。也就是说，对于分支因子较大的游戏和有意义奖励稀疏的游戏，N-CPL 优于 $π$-IW、$π$-IW(1)+ 和 $π$-HIW(n, 1)。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于宽度的新型规划与学习算法，并在Atari-2600游戏基准中验证其有效性，特别强调N-CPL算法的优越性。
AI.motivation: 为解决传统宽度规划算法在分支因子大或奖励稀疏的Atari-2600游戏中表现不足的问题，需设计更高效的规划与学习方法。
AI.method: 提出新型宽度规划算法N-CPL，结合启发式搜索与学习策略，并对Atari-2600游戏按特征进行分类以分析算法适应性。
AI.result: N-CPL算法在多个Atari-2600游戏中超越现有算法，尤其在高分支因子和稀疏奖励场景中表现显著提升。
AI.conclusion: N-CPL算法通过宽度规划与启发式学习结合，有效提升了复杂游戏中的决策性能，游戏分类分析为算法优化提供了理论依据。

title: Better Algorithms for Individually Fair $k$-Clustering
title_zh: 更好的个体公平 $k$ 聚类算法
abstract: We study data clustering problems with $\ell_p$-norm objectives (e.g. $k$-Median and $k$-Means) in the context of individual fairness. The dataset consists of $n$ points, and we want to find $k$ centers such that (a) the objective is minimized, while (b) respecting the individual fairness constraint that every point $v$ has a center within a distance at most $r(v)$, where $r(v)$ is $v$'s distance to its $(n/k)$th nearest point. Jung, Kannan, and Lutz [FORC 2020] introduced this concept and designed a clustering algorithm with provable (approximate) fairness and objective guarantees for the $\ell_\infty$ or $k$-Center objective. Mahabadi and Vakilian [ICML 2020] revisited this problem to give a local-search algorithm for all $\ell_p$-norms. Empirically, their algorithms outperform Jung et. al.'s by a large margin in terms of cost (for $k$-Median and $k$-Means), but they incur a reasonable loss in fairness. In this paper, our main contribution is to use Linear Programming (LP) techniques to obtain better algorithms for this problem, both in theory and in practice. We prove that by modifying known LP rounding techniques, one gets a worst-case guarantee on the objective which is much better than in MV20, and empirically, this objective is extremely close to the optimal. Furthermore, our theoretical fairness guarantees are comparable with MV20 in theory, and empirically, we obtain noticeably fairer solutions. Although solving the LP {\em exactly} might be prohibitive, we demonstrate that in practice, a simple sparsification technique drastically improves the run-time of our algorithm.
summary: We study data clustering problems with $\ell_p$-norm objectives (e.g. $k$-Median and $k$-Means) in the context of individual fairness. The dataset consists of $n$ points, and we want to find $k$ centers such that (a) the objective is minimized, while (b) respecting the individual fairness constraint that every point $v$ has a center within a distance at most $r(v)$, where $r(v)$ is $v$'s distance to its $(n/k)$th nearest point. Jung, Kannan, and Lutz [FORC 2020] introduced this concept and designed a clustering algorithm with provable (approximate) fairness and objective guarantees for the $\ell_\infty$ or $k$-Center objective. Mahabadi and Vakilian [ICML 2020] revisited this problem to give a local-search algorithm for all $\ell_p$-norms. Empirically, their algorithms outperform Jung et. al.'s by a large margin in terms of cost (for $k$-Median and $k$-Means), but they incur a reasonable loss in fairness. In this paper, our main contribution is to use Linear Programming (LP) techniques to obtain better algorithms for this problem, both in theory and in practice. We prove that by modifying known LP rounding techniques, one gets a worst-case guarantee on the objective which is much better than in MV20, and empirically, this objective is extremely close to the optimal. Furthermore, our theoretical fairness guarantees are comparable with MV20 in theory, and empirically, we obtain noticeably fairer solutions. Although solving the LP {\em exactly} might be prohibitive, we demonstrate that in practice, a simple sparsification technique drastically improves the run-time of our algorithm.
abstract_zh: 我们在个人公平的背景下研究具有 $\ell_p$-norm 目标（例如 $k$-Median 和 $k$-Means）的数据聚类问题。数据集由 $n$ 个点组成，我们希望找到 $k$ 个中心，以便 (a) 目标最小化，同时 (b) 尊重个体公平性约束，即每个点 $v$ 的中心距离最多为 $r(v)$，其中 $r(v)$ 是 $v$ 到第 $(n/k)$ 个最近点的距离。 Jung、Kannan 和 Lutz [FORC 2020] 引入了这一概念，并设计了一种聚类算法，具有可证明的（近似）公平性和对 $\ell_\infty$ 或 $k$-Center 目标的客观保证。 Mahabadi 和 Vakilian [ICML 2020] 重新审视了这个问题，为所有 $\ell_p$-范数给出了本地搜索算法。根据经验，他们的算法优于 Jung 等人。就成本而言（对于 $k$-中位数和 $k$-均值），他们有很大优势，但它们在公平性方面造成了合理的损失。在本文中，我们的主要贡献是使用线性规划（LP）技术在理论和实践上获得解决该问题的更好算法。我们证明，通过修改已知的 LP 舍入技术，可以获得比 MV20 更好的最坏情况保证目标，并且根据经验，该目标非常接近最优。此外，我们的理论公平性保证在理论上与 MV20 相当，并且在经验上，我们获得了明显更公平的解决方案。尽管精确地求解线性规划可能令人望而却步，但我们在实践中证明，简单的稀疏化技术可以极大地提高算法的运行时间。

AI.tldr: 本文研究在个体公平约束下的k-聚类问题，提出基于线性规划（LP）的技术改进现有算法，以在理论和实践中实现更优的聚类效果。
AI.motivation: 现有算法在处理ℓ_p范数目标（如k-中位数和k-均值）时，存在成本与公平性之间的权衡问题，需设计兼顾目标函数优化与个体公平性的更高效算法。
AI.method: 通过改进已知的LP取整技术，结合稀疏化技术加速算法运行，设计新的聚类算法以满足个体公平约束并优化目标函数。
AI.result: 理论证明目标函数的最坏情况保证优于先前方法，实际测试显示目标函数接近最优且公平性显著提升，稀疏化技术有效降低计算时间。
AI.conclusion: 基于LP的方法在理论保证和实际效果上均优于现有算法，为个体公平聚类问题提供了更优的解决方案。

We prove that by modifying known LP rounding techniques, one gets a worst-case guarantee on the objective which is much better than in MV20, and empirically, this objective is extremely close to the optimal. Furthermore, our theoretical fairness guarantees are comparable with MV20 in theory, and empirically, we obtain noticeably fairer solutions. Although solving the LP {\em exactly} might be prohibitive, we demonstrate that in practice, a simple sparsification technique drastically improves the run-time of our algorithm. 我们在个人公平的背景下研究具有 $\ell_p$-norm 目标（例如 $k$-Median 和 $k$-Means）的数据聚类问题。数据集由 $n$ 个点组成，我们希望找到 $k$ 个中心，以便 (a) 目标最小化，同时 (b) 尊重个体公平性约束，即每个点 $v$ 的中心距离最多为 $r(v)$，其中 $r(v)$ 是 $v$ 到第 $(n/k)$ 个最近点的距离。 Jung、Kannan 和 Lutz [FORC 2020] 引入了这一概念，并设计了一种聚类算法，具有可证明的（近似）公平性和对 $\ell_\infty$ 或 $k$-Center 目标的客观保证。 Mahabadi 和 Vakilian [ICML 2020] 重新审视了这个问题，为所有 $\ell_p$-范数给出了本地搜索算法。根据经验，他们的算法优于 Jung 等人。就成本而言（对于 $k$-中位数和 $k$-均值），他们有很大优势，但它们在公平性方面造成了合理的损失。在本文中，我们的主要贡献是使用线性规划（LP）技术在理论和实践上获得解决该问题的更好算法。我们证明，通过修改已知的 LP 舍入技术，可以获得比 MV20 更好的最坏情况保证目标，并且根据经验，该目标非常接近最优。此外，我们的理论公平性保证在理论上与 MV20 相当，并且在经验上，我们获得了明显更公平的解决方案。尽管精确地求解线性规划可能令人望而却步，但我们在实践中证明，简单的稀疏化技术可以极大地提高算法的运行时间。 本文研究在个体公平约束下的k-聚类问题，提出基于线性规划（LP）的技术改进现有算法，以在理论和实践中实现更优的聚类效果。 Deeparnab Chakrabarty,Maryam Negahbani cs.DS,cs.CY,cs.LG
